- BNB Chain’s monthly active users (MAU) hit an all-time high of 56.4 million, a 9.5% increase from September 2024’s 51.8 million, driven by new token launches and airdrop campaigns.  \
- Onchain activity surged following rumors of Binance co-founder CZ’s return to Binance, sparking a renewed BSC season.  \
- DEX volumes on BSC reached approximately $24 billion between Sept. 15-21, with a weekly record for 2025, and Aster DEX processed over $4 billion in volume last week.  \
- Aster DEX now dominates the perp DEX space, surpassing Hyperliquid’s $64 billion in volume over the past week, though Hyperliquid maintains higher open interest at $12 billion versus Aster’s $1.3 billion.  \
- Activity on BNB Chain is further boosted by Franklin Templeton expanding its Benji tokenization platform to BNB, and other projects like Swarm and Keeta launching on mainnet.  \
- Overall, onchain activity and trading volumes on BNB Chain are experiencing a significant boom amid renewed investor interest.

news.headline: Keeta Token Rallies on Mainnet Launch
news.summary:
- Keeta’s Layer 1 blockchain officially launched its mainnet on September 22, enabling faster, lower-cost cross-chain transactions.  \
- The native token KTA surged 5% today to $1.11, up 50% since Friday, with a market cap of $447 million.  \
- Mainnet features include asset transfers, swaps, and simplified off-ramping, addressing issues like slow speeds and high fees in older blockchains.  \
- Keeta’s stress test in June recorded 11.2 million TPS, surpassing Solana by 2,500x and exceeding traditional systems like Visa and SWIFT.  \
- The project has raised $22.3 million, with a valuation of $75 million, and launched KTA on Base in March.  \
- Keeta aims to be the most advanced, scalable L1 blockchain, emphasizing DeFi’s need for fast, cross-chain transactions.

news.headline: Nifty Gateway promises permanent NFT hosting in shutdown update
news.summary:
- Nifty Gateway plans to preserve user NFTs after shutting down by migrating most metadata and media to the decentralized storage network Arweave for perpetual hosting.\
- For a subset of NFTs minted in 2021 or earlier, metadata is linked to Nifty Gateway's servers, which the firm pledges to host indefinitely.\
- The Gemini-owned marketplace announced the shutdown last week following a prolonged downturn in the NFT market.\
- The deadline for users to withdraw their NFTs has been extended to April 23, providing a 90-day window.\
- Nifty Gateway is developing a bulk withdrawal tool for users holding multiple NFTs before the April deadline.\
- The platform entered withdrawal-only mode earlier in 2026.

news.headline: Nifty Gateway’s Shutdown Threatens Droves of NFTs
news.summary:
- NFT platform Nifty Gateway has entered withdrawal-only mode, with an official shutdown scheduled for February 23rd, 2026.\
- Collectors have less than a month to retrieve their NFTs, complicated by a mandated Know Your Customer (KYC) process and the lack of batch withdrawal functionality.\
- All NFTs were minted on a single shared "omnibus" smart contract, preventing artists from easily taking over sovereignty.\
- The platform stored the majority of its NFT data on private servers, posing a risk of permanent loss for hundreds of thousands of NFTs if metadata tokenURIs are not updated.\
- An internal proposal reportedly estimated a $7,000 to $8,000 cost to preserve metadata for at least 20 years using IPFS/Arweave, but Nifty Gateway has shown no current plans to implement this solution.\
- MakersPlace provided a favorable contrast during its closure by offering extended notice, batch transfers, and covering gas costs for users.

news.headline: Australia classifies stablecoins, wrapped tokens as financial products in updated guidance
news.summary:
- ASIC updates crypto guidance, now classifying stablecoins, wrapped tokens, tokenized securities, and digital wallets as financial products in Australia.  \
- Companies offering these assets must obtain a local financial services license to operate legally.  \
- ASIC grants a no-action relief until June 30, 2026, giving industry time to comply and seek licenses.  \
- The move follows months of consultation and aligns with recent legislative proposals to clarify crypto regulation.  \
- In September, ASIC eased licensing rules for stablecoin intermediaries via a class exemption; Treasury also proposed licensing requirements in October.  \
- These measures aim to strengthen consumer protections and establish clearer regulatory standards for digital assets in Australia.

news.headline: SafePal and 1inch to Give Away Hardware Wallets to Boost DeFi Security
news.summary:
- SafePal and 1inch are launching a free hardware wallet giveaway to reward active DeFi users and promote self-custody.  \
- 300 limited-edition, co-branded wallets will be distributed to users who trade via 1inch within SafePal’s mobile wallet swap aggregator.  \
- The campaign aims to target committed DeFi users, avoiding typical token airdrop pitfalls like bot gaming and short-term engagement.  \
- DeFi’s market share hit a record 30% of global crypto spot trading volume in June, highlighting growing peer-to-peer trading interest.  \
- SafePal recently upgraded its hardware wallets’ security chips, added new blockchain integrations, and launched products like SafePal Mini.  \
- 1inch emphasizes the importance of self-custody as DeFi expands, especially after past centralized exchange failures.

news.headline: Coinbase loses $300,000 to MEV bots following 0x swapper interaction: security researcher
news.summary:
- Coinbase lost around $300,000 in token fees due to interaction with a 0x Project swapper contract on August 14, 2025.  \
- No customer funds were affected, according to Coinbase CSO Philip Martin, who called it an isolated incident.  \
- The loss resulted from a misconfigured approval process with the permissionless 0x swapper contract, which was exploited by MEV bots.  \
- Security researcher "deeberiroz" identified that Coinbase approved tokens like Amp, MyOneProtocol, DEXTools, and Swell Network, enabling bots to drain tokens.  \
- The incident was confirmed by Coinbase, which revoked token allowances and moved funds to a new corporate wallet.  \
- The event highlights risks of permissionless smart contracts and the importance of proper configuration to prevent MEV bot exploits.

news.headline: Amp up Patriot Act to curb criminal use of decentralised blockchains, says private watchdog
Move-to-Earn Game ‘Stepn Go’ Reveals G-Shock NFT Sneaker Collab
Gaming Projects Set to Unlock $175 Million in Tokens Within Weeks
news.summary:
- Several gaming tokens, including SAND, PRIME, APE, IMX, YGG, and GMT, are set to unlock a total of $175 million worth of tokens by the end of February.\
- The unlocks could impact the market price of these tokens, as they increase the circulating supply.\
- The largest unlock is expected for SAND on February 2, with 205.59 million tokens unlocking, representing about $99.2 million worth of SAND.\
- Other tokens, such as APE, IMX, PRIME, YGG, and GMT, will also have unlocks throughout February, ranging from $7.89 million to $25 million worth of tokens.\
- Crypto traders pay attention to unlock dates as a large dump of tokens could signal sell pressure and impact the token's price.\
- Despite the unlocks, some gaming tokens have seen significant gains recently, such as IMX, ApeCoin, and PRIME.

news.headline: AI Sector Rebounds as Agent Payment Systems Gain Traction
news.summary:
- AI coins, led by Virtuals and x402 protocols, are experiencing a strong rebound, with Virtuals up 84% last week and key tokens like TIBBIR and AIXBT up 27% and 54%.  \
- Coinbase's recent support for Virtuals-based AI agent tokens on October 24 helped catalyze the rally.  \
- Virtuals' token surged over 10,000% from October 2024 to January 2025, reaching $5, before retracing to $1.46 today.  \
- x402 protocols, enabling AI agent payment systems, are seeing explosive growth, with total transactions rising from 60,000 in early October to 1.51 million now.  \
- PayAI’s token has surged 2000% over the past month, reflecting increased interest in AI-led finance.  \
- Industry experts warn that much of the recent speculative gains may not be sustainable, emphasizing that only projects close to commercialization are likely to survive long-term.

news.headline: Morning Minute: Coinbase Buys Cobie's Echo \u0026 Up Only NFT for $375M
Tucker Carlson casts Roger Ver as victim ‘silenced’ by US government
Consensys-backed SharpLink stakes $170 million in ETH on Linea
news.summary:
- SharpLink Gaming deployed and staked $170 million in Ether (ETH) on Consensys's Layer 2 blockchain, Linea, to optimize yield.\
- The structure layers native Ethereum staking yield with restaking rewards from EigenCloud, plus direct incentives from Linea and Ether.fi, using custody from Anchorage.\
- This deployment is part of SharpLink’s broader plan to stake $200 million across Linea and restaking partners.\
- CEO Joseph Chalom stated that 2026 signals the beginning of Ethereum's "productive era."\
- SharpLink currently holds 864,840 ETH, valued around $2.7 billion, making it the second-largest corporate ETH treasury.\
- Separately, Bitmine reportedly staked an additional 186,304 ETH ($577.5 million) on Thursday, bringing its total staked ETH to approximately 1.03 million.

news.headline: Ethereum Treasury Firm SharpLink Stakes $170 Million of ETH on Linea
news.summary:
- Publicly traded Ethereum treasury firm SharpLink Gaming deployed $170 million worth of ETH onto the Linea layer-2 network on Thursday, January 08, 2026.\
- This deployment is part of a previously announced multi-year effort to stake up to $200 million on Linea to optimize on-chain yields.\
- SharpLink, which holds 864,840 ETH valued near $2.7 billion, will earn re-staking rewards from Eigen Cloud and incentives from EtherFi in addition to native ETH staking yields.\
- The move is intended to generate additional yield "in excess of its current staking rewards" while pushing institutional DeFi adoption, according to CIO Matt Sheffield.\
- Shares of SharpLink (SBET) rose 1.4% on Thursday but remain down approximately 37% over the last six months.\
- SharpLink is a member of the Linea Consortium, which helps manage the distribution of the LINEA token.

news.headline: Apollo Launches $785 Billion AUM Tokenized Credit Fund ACRED on Solana DeFi With Drift Institutional and Kamino Finance
news.summary:
- Apollo Global Management is launching a $785 billion AUM tokenized credit fund, ACRED, on the Solana blockchain.  \
- The fund is facilitated through partnerships with Kamino Finance, Securitize, and SteakhouseFi, and will be issued as a regulated sToken by Securitize.  \
- ACRED aims to enable borrowing, leverage, and DeFi strategies for real-world assets (RWA) on Solana, marking its first RWA offering.  \
- The launch is pending an audit completion.  \
- Drift Protocol introduced Drift Institutional, a service to help large institutions bring RWAs on-chain within Solana's DeFi ecosystem.  \
- Drift Institutional's first offering includes an Institutional Pool for Apollo’s tokenized credit fund, integrating ACRED into Solana DeFi.

news.headline: Apollo’s Tokenized Credit Fund Set for Solana DeFi Debut as RWA Trend Expands
news.summary:
- Kamino Finance plans to launch ACRED, a tokenized version of Apollo Global’s private credit fund, on Solana’s DeFi ecosystem, pending an audit.  \
- ACRED, issued under Securitize’s regulated token framework, will be the first on Solana to enable on-chain borrowing and leverage of RWAs.  \
- The move highlights growing interest in tokenizing real-world assets like funds and bonds to enhance DeFi yield strategies and access.  \
- Currently, Solana hosts $330M in RWAs, significantly less than its $9B DeFi market and Ethereum’s $7B RWA market, but industry insiders see a potential tipping point.  \
- Kamino’s Multiply product will allow users to leverage ACRED for yield farming and automated investment strategies, similar to offerings on other chains.  \
- Industry leaders see tokenization of RWAs as a key driver for long-term DeFi growth on Solana and beyond.

news.headline: Zcash Leads Privacy Coin Surge Amid Surveillance Fears
news.summary:
- Zcash (ZEC) surged 48% last week to over $268, its highest since April 2022, amid a sector-wide rally for privacy coins like Railgun and Dash.  \
- The rally is driven by increasing concerns over financial surveillance, sanctions, and geopolitical uncertainty, prompting investors to seek privacy-focused assets.  \
- Other privacy coins such as Veil Token (+248%), Railgun (+225%), PIVX (+106%), and BEAM (+73%) also saw significant gains; Monero remained flat.  \
- Grayscale launched a Zcash trust, and Railgun’s total value locked more than doubled, signaling rising user activity and momentum.  \
- Experts note that privacy coins tend to rally during times of economic uncertainty and increased surveillance fears, with institutional and retail investor interest growing.  \
- Bitcoin pulled back slightly to around $121,349 after hitting nearly $125,000 over the weekend, as markets digest the U.S. government shutdown.

news.headline: New chart, who dis? Token rebrands might work only once
news.summary:
- Token rebrands in the crypto world are becoming more common, serving to refresh market positioning, create promotional buzz, and reset price charts.\
- Notable recent rebrands include merit circle (MC) to BEAM, bitdao (BIT) to MANTLE (MNT), matic to POL, makerdao (MKR) to SKY, and fantom (FTM) to SONIC (S).\
- These rebrands often result in separate price charts for old and new tickers, which can obscure the true historical performance of the tokens.\
- For instance, BEAM appears up 70% post-rebrand but is actually down 70% over its entire history, while MNT is up 56% since its rebrand but down 50% from its original launch.\
- POL has decreased 56% since its rebrand but boasts nearly 5,000% lifetime returns, and MKR/SKY is up 50% over its entire history despite only a 4% increase post-rebrand.\
- The effectiveness of rebranding may be limited, as it might only work once to rejuvenate a token's market presence.

news.headline: Do crypto teams still need offshore foundations?
news.summary:
- US regulatory shifts are making offshore crypto foundations, like those in the Cayman Islands, less necessary, with experts citing high costs and legal challenges.  \
- Founders argue foundations no longer boost decentralization and are often costly, slow, and difficult to set up, especially for small startups.  \
- Many attorneys believe token issuances no longer require foundations unless for specific tax benefits or jurisdictional reasons; US-based alternatives like UNAs or LLCs are recommended.  \
- US teams launching tokens may still prefer Cayman foundations for tax reasons, but US regulation progress could reduce this need in the future.  \
- Livepeer recently launched a foundation to navigate current regulations, but some see foundations as bloated intermediaries that hinder decentralization.  \
- Overall, the trend suggests foundations may become less central, encouraging leaner structures and increased competition for crypto startups.

news.headline: AI Crypto Livepeer Explodes 150% on Upbit Listing
news.summary:
- Livepeer's native token LPT surged 150% to a four-month high of $14.20 after being listed on Korean exchange Upbit.  \
- The rally defied broader market downturns, with Bitcoin dropping 2% and the CoinDesk 20 Index falling 3.5%.  \
- Grayscale added Livepeer to its new Artificial Intelligence Crypto Sector, and a community survey by Livepeer Foundation may have boosted momentum.  \
- The token's spike was driven by Upbit's trading debut against KRW and USDT earlier on Friday.  \
- Other AI-focused tokens declined 5-10%, highlighting Livepeer's unique performance amid market weakness.  \
- Livepeer is a decentralized AI video processing protocol, with recent catalysts including sector inclusion and community engagement.

news.headline: One Year On: Inside ApeChain’s Brief Rise and Slow Fall
news.summary:
- ApeChain’s TVL has plummeted over 80% since December 2024, now at $6.7 million, with only 3 of nearly 30 protocols generating revenue.  \
- ApeCoin (APE) has fallen over 95% from its launch price of $0.87, trading at $0.36 today, reducing its market cap from $629M to around $325M.  \
- The project’s governance shifted from a DAO to Yuga Labs’ ApeCo in June, with 99.66% support, aiming to accelerate ecosystem development.  \
- Broader market trends, token unlocks, and a sluggish NFT market have contributed to ApeChain’s decline, mirroring patterns seen across other Layer 2s and ecosystems.  \
- Yuga Labs has shifted focus to core IP and metaverse projects, selling assets like CryptoPunks and Meebits, amid NFT market struggles and a recent crypto downturn.  \
- Despite regulatory clarity after the SEC ended its investigation in March, ApeCoin and the NFT sector have yet to see a significant recovery, with NFT volumes down over 44% in October.

news.headline: Morning Minute: a16z’s 'State of Crypto 2025' Highlights Growth \u0026 Key Trends
news.summary:
- a16z’s "State of Crypto 2025" report highlights continued growth in the crypto sector, with increased institutional adoption and expanding use cases.  \
- Bitcoin (BTC) remains near $110,000, with modest 0.31% daily gains, while Ethereum (ETH) is up 1.62% at around $3,906.  \
- DeFi and layer-1 protocols like Solana (SOL), Avalanche (AVAX), and Near (NEAR) show steady activity, with some tokens like APE and JUP surging over 19%.  \
- Stablecoins such as USDC and USDT maintain peg stability, though some tokens like TRX and HYPE experienced declines.  \
- The report emphasizes the maturation of crypto infrastructure, increased regulatory clarity, and growing mainstream acceptance.  \
- Overall market sentiment remains cautiously optimistic, with key trends pointing toward broader adoption and technological innovation in 2025.

news.headline: Pavel Durov laughs at WhatsApp, but Telegram is just as opaque
news.summary:
- Telegram founder Pavel Durov mocked WhatsApp's security following a lawsuit alleging Meta can access user messages, despite Telegram also having security flaws.\
- WhatsApp vigorously denied the lawsuit's allegations, calling the claims about message encryption false and the suit frivolous.\
- Researchers have previously identified critical bugs in Telegram allowing plaintext retrieval of encrypted messages and man-in-the-middle attacks.\
- Telegram has faced scrutiny for lax enforcement on channels selling illicit goods and the company's ability to access non-secret chats.\
- Elon Musk's advertised XChat faced immediate fact-checking from Community Notes debunking its security claims and highlighting vulnerabilities.\
- Several cryptocurrency projects affiliated with Donald Trump, including the $TRUMP memecoin and Melania Trump’s $MELANIA token, have experienced significant value collapse since his inauguration.

news.headline: The Context 157: 💭 Forgotten Trump Crypto Projects
news.summary:
- The $TRUMP memecoin has fallen approximately 88% in price since Donald Trump's inauguration, reaching about $4.93 from over $42.\
- World Liberty Financial, which aligned with $TRUMP, failed to launch its promised Aave V3 instance despite a 99% tokenholder vote approving it in December 2024.\
- The $MELANIA memecoin has seen a near-total collapse, trading down 98% from $7.42 at inauguration to $0.15 currently.\
- Melania Trump's NFT projects have faced criticism, including one that allegedly involved wash-trading and another that reportedly violated NASA photo rules.\
- ALT5 Sigma, partnered with World Liberty Financial, fired its CEO and COO amid a money laundering inquiry in Rwanda and was subsequently added to NASDAQ’s list of "noncompliant companies."\
- American Bitcoin Corporation, where Eric Trump is Chief Strategy Officer, saw its stock price fall from $6.82 at its September 2025 reverse merger to $1.55.

Aethir and Credible Introduce DePIN-Powered Credit Card
news.summary:
- Aethir and Credible Finance launch the first DePIN-powered credit card and loan product, allowing eligible users to access stablecoin credit without liquidating ATH tokens.  \
- The product, launched on July 16, enables collateralization of ATH tokens for revolving credit lines or no-fee cards on Solana, with approval based on Credible’s AI credit engine.  \
- DePIN projects like Aethir leverage blockchain incentives to crowdsource real-world infrastructure, rewarding node operators with ATH based on performance.  \
- The move comes amid a crypto lending downturn, with Q1 seeing a $4.75 billion decline in open loans, highlighting limitations of overcollateralized models.  \
- Credible CEO Shrikant Bhalerao emphasizes turning tokenized infrastructure into real-time financial capital, with initial access limited to GPU providers, node operators, and token holders.  \
- The team plans to expand credit access later this year as DePIN infrastructure integrates more deeply with on-chain finance.

news.headline: Starknet Partners with NEAR to Bring STRK to Solana
news.summary:
- Starknet's native token, STRK, is now natively accessible on the Solana ecosystem via an integration facilitated by NEAR Intents as of January 15, 2026.\
- The integration allows users to receive STRK directly into Solana wallets without needing a standard bridging process, utilizing NEAR Intents' solver-based execution model.\
- STRK will be available for spot trading on the Solana DEX Jupiter, with Meteora designated as the primary liquidity venue for the token on Solana.\
- This move follows a public jab from Solana's official X account on January 14, 2026, criticizing Starknet's reported low daily activity and valuation.\
- Starknet's Total Value Locked (TVL) has surpassed $313 million, representing an increase of over $100 million since December 2025.\
- At press time, STRK is trading at $0.086, holding a market capitalization of approximately $435 million.

news.headline: Morning Minute: VanEck Leans Into 'Degen' Culture with New ETF
news.summary:
- VanEck is converting its existing Gaming ETF into the VanEck Degen Economy ETF, set to launch in April 2026.\
- The new ETF will track public companies generating at least 50% of revenue from Millennial Finance, Gig Economy & Online Forums, or Digital Gambling, Betting & Gaming sectors.\
- The fund's structure mirrors consumer trends in mobile finance, gig work, and digital betting, reflecting shifts in post-Covid consumer behavior.\
- Crypto majors tumbled following Fed Chair Powell's expected 25 basis point interest rate cut, with BTC dropping 2% to $90,250.\
- Democrats countered with a 3-page set of changes after accepting "significant portions" of the market structure bill.\
- Meteora announced $10 million in MET token buybacks and plans to continue discretionary buybacks.

news.headline: 'Nyan Heroes' Plots Epic Games Store Return With New Hero, Map and Mode
Bhutan Pledges 10,000 Bitcoin Worth $1B to Fund Mindfulness City
news.summary:
- Two former senior executives, Jerry Kowal and Andrea Berry, filed separate whistleblower lawsuits against blockchain firm Theta Labs and CEO Mitch Liu in California.\
- The lawsuits allege a years-long pattern of deception, token manipulation (including "pump-and-dump" schemes), and retaliation against employees who raised concerns.\
- Allegations include Liu using Theta Labs to inflate token prices through misleading partnerships and undisclosed insider token sales.\
- One complaint claims Theta misrepresented a routine cloud services agreement with Google, announced in May 2020, as a strategic partnership to boost validation.\
- The filings also detail alleged abuses involving generating false bids for non-fungible tokens (NFTs), some linked to celebrity partnerships.\
- The plaintiffs characterize the activity as self-dealing tied to Theta’s THETA and TFUEL tokens and its NFT marketplace.

news.headline: Former Theta Executives Accuse Crypto Firm's CEO of Fraud, Retaliation
news.summary:
- Former Theta executives Jerry Kowal and Andrea Berry filed separate whistleblower lawsuits in Los Angeles Superior Court against Theta Labs and CEO Mitch Liu.\
- Allegations include a pattern of deception, market manipulation, and retaliation against employees who raised concerns.\
- Lawsuits claim Liu inflated token prices through misleading partnerships and undisclosed insider token sales, constituting alleged "pump-and-dump" schemes.\
- Berry’s complaint specifically alleges that a publicized 2020 "partnership" with Google was misrepresented, amounting only to a routine cloud services agreement where Theta was a customer.\
- Plaintiffs characterize the conduct as self-dealing tied to Theta’s THETA and TFUEL tokens and its NFT marketplace, including generating false bids for NFTs.\
- One allegation suggests Liu used Theta Labs as a "personal trading vehicle" to enrich himself.

- Tokenize Xchange, based in Singapore, is a digital asset exchange with a 24-hour trading volume of over $250 million, serving both retail and institutional clients.\
- Following the acquisition announcement, Tokenize's native token, TKX, saw a decline of 8.2% in the last 24 hours, underperforming the broader market.\
- The acquisition aims to bolster Tokenize's technological infrastructure and provide advanced insights for its users.

Crypto Daybook Americas: Roses Are Red, Violets Are Blue, Inflation Is Hot, but Bitcoin Shines Through
news.summary:
- Bitcoin has surged past $97,000 despite higher-than-expected U.S. inflation reports, defying expectations that risky assets would pause amid potential Fed rate cuts.\
- Analysts suggest Bitcoin's resilience may be due to expectations of easing inflation, with the Truflation U.S. Inflation Index indicating a potential decline to 2.06%.\
- Historical trends and the 200-week moving average suggest the Bitcoin bull market could continue, with short-term holders accumulating 1.5 million BTC since September.\
- On the corporate front, Coinbase reported strong earnings, and Gamestop is considering a Bitcoin investment, potentially boosting market sentiment.\
- Upcoming crypto events include network upgrades for Dynamic TAO and Qtum, and Ethereum's Pectra upgrade testing, alongside significant token unlocks for Starknet, Sei, Arbitrum, and others.\
- In the broader market, Bitcoin's dominance is slightly down, while Ethereum and other major indices like the S&P 500 and Nasdaq have seen gains.

news.headline: Aptos Jumps 10% Overnight as South Korea Trading Volumes Spike
ARK Invest Swaps Nearly $9M of Its Own Bitcoin ETF for Coinbase
news.summary:
- ARK Invest, led by Cathie Wood, purchased $8.7 million worth of Coinbase (COIN) shares, adding 41,032 shares to its Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW) as COIN's stock dropped 6.5% to $212.49.\
- Simultaneously, ARK sold 98,060 shares of its own spot bitcoin ETF (ARKB), valued at approximately $8.6 million.\
- The cryptocurrency market experienced a significant downturn, with bitcoin (BTC) falling to a three-month low, leading to record outflows from U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs.\
- Spot bitcoin ETFs saw net outflows of nearly $940 million, marking the largest single-day sale since their inception in January 2024, as reported by SoSoValue.\
- Despite the market slide, ARK's purchase of COIN shares was its largest in dollar terms since August 5, when it acquired 93,800 shares for $17.8 million.

news.headline: Zcash Sentiment Ticks Up as Privacy Coin's Price Jumps 9%
news.summary:
- Zcash (ZEC) price surged 9% in the last 24 hours, reaching $376.90 to $381.\
- Sentiment on the Myriad prediction market shows 40% of users now anticipate ZEC hitting $550 before $250, an 8% increase in optimistic expectations from Monday.\
- Despite recent gains, Zcash remains down over 26% month-on-month following mass executive resignations at the Electric Coin Company on January 8.\
- Internal turmoil, stemming from a potential for-profit transition disagreement, has led to offshoot projects like CashZ, led by former CEO Josh Swihart.\
- The Zcash Foundation confirmed on Wednesday that the U.S. SEC ended its probe into the organization without recommending enforcement action, a review that began in August 2023.\
- The broader privacy coin sector is up 8.8% in the past 24 hours, with Verge (XVG), DASH, and Monero (XMR) also seeing gains.

news.headline: Monero’s 51% Attack Problem: Inside Qubic’s Controversial Network Takeover
Infinex Token Launches at $300 Million Valuation
news.summary:
- Infinex, a DeFi product suite and super app led by Synthetix founder Kain Warwick, conducted its INX token generation event (TGE) today.\
- The INX token debuted at a $330 million fully diluted valuation, trading at $0.033, but subsequently dropped to $0.021 as early investors took profits.\
- Infinex functions as a decentralized super app connecting dApps across over 25 blockchains, covering roughly 85% of the top 100 assets listed on CoinGecko.\
- The protocol raised $7.2 million in its Initial Coin Offering (ICO), which concluded on January 10, 2026.\
- An earlier Patron NFT mint in 2024 generated over $67 million in sales, with NFT holders claiming 100,000 INX tokens each.\
- The platform aims to integrate prediction markets and NFT marketplaces, offering users a single login across its underlying applications like Infinex Perps and Infinex Predict.

RWA stablecoin issuer Anzen Finance raises $4 million in seed funding

- Participating investors include Mechanism Capital, Circle Ventures, Frax, Arca, and others.\
- Anzen allows users to mint USDz, which is collateralized by a portfolio of private credit assets.\

- Anzen users can earn rewards through various dApps, such as the USDz-USDC liquidity pair on Aerodrome.\
- The market capitalization for RWA-aligned tokens has grown 64% in the past two months, reaching $9.05 billion.

news.headline: U.S. inflation report, BNB Smart Chain’s hard fork: Crypto Week Ahead
news.summary:
- Key economic focus this week is the U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) data release on Tuesday, with expectations for headline YoY inflation to hold steady at 2.7%.\
- BNB Smart Chain is scheduled to implement its Fermi hard fork on Tuesday, aiming to reduce block times by 40% for improved network speed.\
- Hedera (HBAR) is set for a mainnet upgrade to v0.68 on January 13 at 1 p.m. ET.\
- Mantle (MNT) is planning a mainnet upgrade (v.1.4.2) on January 14 at 2 a.m. ET, focused on supporting Ethereum's "Fusaka" upgrade features.\

- The U.S. Senate Banking Committee is set to markup the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act on January 15.

news.headline: Wintermute’s post-mortem ignored the biggest issues with Friday’s crash
news.summary:
- Wintermute founder Evgeny Gaevoy claimed the firm was unaffected by Friday’s crypto crash, calling it “business as usual,” despite widespread market turmoil.  \
- The crash saw tokens like Cosmos and Ravencoin plummet 99.9% on Binance, and Ethena’s USDe stablecoin de-peg from $1 for an hour, with $600 billion in assets lost within 30 minutes.  \
- Wintermute’s post-mortem downplayed the severity, citing “60-80% at the peak” losses based on aggregated data, ignoring the actual 99.9% flash crashes on Binance.  \
- The analysis also omitted the hour-long de-peg of Ethena’s USDe and reports of Wintermute depositing approximately $700 million into Binance before the crash.  \
- Gaevoy dismissed concerns about market manipulation and criticized traders’ losses, while acknowledging USDe was not truly pegged and may have received preferential treatment on exchanges.  \
- Wintermute did not respond to inquiries about its Binance deposits, the flash crashes, or the USDe de-peg as of publication.

news.headline: Andre Cronje’s Flying Tulip Gears Up for Public Sale
news.summary:
- Flying Tulip, the latest DeFi project from Yearn Finance founder Andre Cronje, will conduct its public token sale on February 16, with the token going live on February 23.\
- Prediction markets assign a 50-50 chance for the Flying Tulip token to trade above a $400 million fully diluted valuation (FDV) immediately post-launch, noted to be on low volumes.\
- Flying Tulip aims to be a DeFi "super application," consolidating perpetual derivatives trading, spot trading, and lending on one platform, similar to Infinex.\
- Cronje stated that the Flying Tulip FDV calculation is non-standard, incorporating an underlying put option, making its valuation closer to Net Asset Value (NAV).\
- The protocol secured substantial funding, including a $200 million raise in September and a recent $10 million raise via CoinList last week.\
- Cronje's previous major venture, the Layer-1 blockchain Fantom (now Sonic), is trading 96% down from its launch price.

news.headline: Legacy DeFi platforms lose $27M as hacking spree continues into 2026
Crypto Custody Firm Copper’s CEO Dmitry Tokarev Plans to Step Down
news.summary:
- Copper CEO Dmitry Tokarev plans to step down from his role to assume a founder's position within the company, remaining a significant shareholder.\
- The firm is actively interviewing candidates to replace Tokarev, who has led Copper since its inception in 2018.\
- Recent executive departures include Boris Bohrer-Bilowitzki and Mike Milner, who both left to join blockchain firm Concordium.\
- Copper, a digital assets custodian, counts former U.K. Chancellor Philip Hammond as its chairman.\
- A Copper spokesperson declined to comment on the CEO transition, labeling it as market speculation.

news.headline: From spot bitcoin ETFs to real-world assets, industry leaders share their crypto outlook for 2024
Nick Shirley token highlights Base’s Zora-driven surge and vanity metric issue
news.summary:
- The number of tokens launched on Base launchpads recently peaked at over 100,000 in a single day, largely due to Zora and its content coin mechanism.\
- This creation surge contrasts with Base seeing 18-month lows in active addresses and declining transaction volume, signaling a "vanity metric" problem.\
- Zora's low-cost token deployment allows for rapid minting, resulting in a flood of low-value tokens that inflate creation stats without driving sustained economic activity.\
- Content creator Nick Shirley’s $thenickshirley token, launched following viral coverage of a fraud investigation, peaked at a $15 million market cap but has since collapsed to $74,900.\
- Shirley earned an estimated $40,000 to $65,000 in creator royalties from the speculative trading but provided no sustained engagement or community strategy for holders.\
- The failure of high-profile Zora tokens like Shirley's underscores that they currently act primarily as vehicles for "speculation on speculation" lacking fundamental value.

news.headline: Base’s creator coin push sparks builder backlash over favoritism concerns
news.summary:
- Base builders are criticizing the network for its heavy promotion of "creator coins" primarily through its close alignment with NFT marketplace Zora.\
- Critics argue this narrow focus sidelines established, native Base projects that previously contributed to the network's momentum.\
- Jacek, running a degen token ecosystem on Base, cited a lack of official social media support (retweets, follows) unless tied to Zora initiatives, contrasting it with support received on rival chains like Solana.\
- Base continues to process over 10 million transactions daily, but declining builder sentiment raises concerns about project migration to rivals like Solana or Sui.\
- The surge in creator tokens on Base, amplified by Zora, led to daily token mints surpassing Solana in August of this year.\
- The backlash represents a tension for Base in balancing scaling adoption with maintaining positive relationships across its entire builder community.

news.headline: Hacker swipes $17m from Matcha Meta users as protocol reports security ‘incident’
news.summary:
- A hacker stole nearly $17 million in crypto from users of Matcha Meta, a DeFi exchange meta aggregator built by 0x, starting around 5:10 PM London time on January 25.\
- Matcha Meta attributed the security incident to SwapNet, an integrated exchange aggregator, confirming the attack at 9:47 PM.\
- Affected users were those whose trades routed through SwapNet and who had disabled the protocol's One-Time Approval feature.\
- Users were advised to immediately revoke all external approvals outside of 0x’s One-Time Approval contracts as a precautionary measure.\
- The exploit appears to be an "approval attack," where an attacker drained an open allowance to a specific contract, described as the largest such attack seen excluding phishing.\
- The incident was not associated with 0x’s AllowanceHolder or Settler contracts, according to the protocol.

news.headline: Matcha Meta sees $16.8 million drained in SwapNet incident: PeckShield
news.summary:
- Matcha Meta disclosed a security incident involving its SwapNet integration on Sunday, January 26, 2026.\
- PeckShield estimated that approximately $16.8 million in crypto assets were drained from the platform.\
- The attacker reportedly swapped about $10.5 million in USDC on Base for roughly 3,655 ETH before bridging the funds to Ethereum.\
- The exposure was limited to users who disabled One-Time Approvals and instead set direct allowances on individual aggregator contracts.\
- Matcha Meta confirmed the issue was not related to 0x's AllowanceHolder or Settler contracts.\
- The project has since removed the ability for users to set direct allowances on aggregators to prevent future occurrences.

news.headline: Spark airdrop lights the fuse on decentralized governance
The State of DAO M\u0026A
Inflation? Justin Sun Eats Banana He Bought for $6.2 Million
news.summary:
- **Banana Art**: Tron founder Justin Sun ate a banana he bought for $6.2 million, part of Maurizio Cattelan's conceptual artwork "Comedian," at a press event in Hong Kong.\
- **Art Meets Crypto**: Sun drew parallels between conceptual art and cryptocurrency, highlighting the meme culture surrounding both.\
- **Meme Coins Surge**: Following Sun's stunt, two Solana meme coins related to the artwork, Banana Tape Wall (BTW) and Comedian (Ban), saw significant price increases.\
- **Investment Moves**: Sun announced a $30 million investment in the Trump-family backed World Liberty Financial (WLFI) project, which aims to launch a borrowing and lending crypto service on Ethereum.\
- **Stablecoin Plans**: The WLFI project plans to issue a stablecoin pegged to the U.S. dollar, furthering its financial services ambitions.

news.headline: Justin Sun enters the banana zone with $6.2 million purchase of ‘Comedian’ artwork
news.summary:
- Tron founder Justin Sun purchased Maurizio Cattelan’s artwork “Comedian” for $6.24 million at a Sotheby’s auction in New York.\
- The artwork, a banana duct-taped to a wall, was initially estimated to sell for $1 million to $1.5 million but saw intense bidding.\
- Sun announced the purchase on X, highlighting the piece's cultural significance bridging art, memes, and cryptocurrency.\
- Following the purchase, the largest Comedian-themed memecoin dropped 37%, with the Solana-based Comedian token (Ban) falling from a market cap of $241 million to $151 million.\
- Sun plans to eat the banana as part of the artistic experience, continuing a tradition started by performance artists in 2019 and 2023.

news.headline: Thai SEC Fines Bitkub Executive $235K for Insider Trading
Thailand’s SCB abandons $500 million deal to buy crypto exchange Bitkub
Crypto Traders Eye Major Events to Relieve Market Woes: Crypto Week Ahead
news.summary:
- Crypto markets remain volatile after liquidations between Oct. 10-17, with BTC and ETH trading below early October highs.  \
- Key upcoming events include ETHZilla’s 1-for-10 reverse stock split on Oct. 22 and a major Zilliqa upgrade on Oct. 24.  \
- The Federal Reserve’s payments conference on Oct. 21 is closely watched for hints on future monetary policy.  \
- Macro data releases this week include Canada’s inflation and PPI reports, and U.S. housing and inflation data, though some reports are delayed due to government shutdown.  \
- Bitcoin surged past $111K amid bullish on-chain signals; XRP, SOL, and ETH also rallied, supported by positive macro factors like China’s GDP and falling dollar index.  \
- Stablecoin payment volumes hit $19.4B in 2025, with companies like OwlTing developing faster, cheaper transaction infrastructure.

news.headline: StraitsX Launches Its Singapore-Dollar Pegged Stablecoin, XSGD, on XRP Ledger
news.summary:
- StraitsX launched its Singapore dollar-pegged stablecoin, XSGD, on the XRP Ledger to support cross-border payments and multi-chain interoperability.  \
- XSGD is fully backed 1:1 by reserves at DBS Bank and Standard Chartered, with a total supply of 14.12 million and over 8 billion on-chain transactions.  \
- The stablecoin is available on multiple blockchains including Arbitrum, Avalanche, Ethereum, Polygon, Hedera, and Zilliqa.  \
- The rollout on XRP Ledger is part of a strategic partnership, with plans to expand institutional features like programmable payouts and merchant settlements in June.  \
- Ripple’s XRP Ledger is seen as a key infrastructure for regulated stablecoins to facilitate real-time, low-cost cross-border transactions amid rising global economic activity.  \
- Ripple and StraitsX emphasize stablecoins like XSGD as vital for future financial markets, supporting enterprise use cases and compliance-driven payments.

news.headline: What Coinbase listings say about crypto in the US
news.summary:
- Coinbase has significantly increased its rate of listings, with 16 new announcements since Trump's election win, compared to an average of 3.8 per month over the past two years.\
- Seven of the new listings are popular memecoins like WIF, PEPE, and FLOKI, which have strong followings and have been long-requested by users.\
- Coinbase's recent memecoin listings, including MOODENG, mark a shift from its previous cautious approach, having only listed one memecoin, BONK, in the past two years.\
- The exchange has also added more substantive coins like Helium’s MOBILE, DEGEN, ARB, SEI, AIOZ, 1INCH, EGLD, and SD, particularly for New York residents.\
- The move to list more memecoins is seen as a strategy to boost trading volume and fee revenue, though its impact on Coinbase’s valuation remains uncertain.\
- Despite the increased listings, Coinbase’s share price has closely tracked Bitcoin’s performance and has slightly underperformed in recent weeks.

news.headline: What is DePIN?
news.summary:
- DePIN (Decentralized Physical Infrastructure Network) is a concept that leverages blockchain technology to decentralize control and ownership of physical infrastructure.\
- DePIN projects aim to create peer-to-peer networks where individuals contribute their physical resources and have shared control over the network.\
- DePIN projects promote community participation by giving users decision-making authority and cryptocurrency rewards.\
- Use cases of DePIN include decentralized finance, data storage, telecommunications, transportation, and healthcare.\
- Well-known DePIN projects include Filecoin, Render, Livepeer, Akash Network, Helium, AIOZ, and Hivemapper.\
- DePIN has the potential to benefit industries like mobility and AI by providing scalable and agile infrastructure. However, challenges include interoperability, scalability, safety, and verification of participants.

news.headline: Tether to Halt USDT on Omni, BCH, Kusama, EOS, Algorand as Focus Shifts to Layer 2s
news.summary:
- Tether will cease support for USDT on Omni, BCH, Kusama, EOS, and Algorand on September 1, 2025, due to declining usage over the past two years.  \
- Remaining tokens on these networks will be frozen, and redemptions or minting will stop on that date.  \
- The five networks currently hold only a small fraction of USDT’s $156 billion total supply, with most USDT circulating on Tron and Ethereum.  \
- Tether is shifting focus to Layer 2 solutions like the Lightning Network and newer blockchains with better scalability and developer activity.  \
- The move aims to support the next wave of stablecoin adoption by focusing on platforms with greater community engagement.  \
- Customers are encouraged to redeem or migrate their USDT holdings via bridges or exchanges before the support ends.

news.headline: Tether to sunset USDT on five legacy blockchains, including Algorand and Omni
news.summary:
- Tether will sunset USDT support on five legacy blockchains—Algorand, Bitcoin Cash, EOS, Kusama, and Omni—starting September 1, 2025, to focus on scalable platforms with higher developer activity.  \
- Redemptions on these chains will cease, and remaining tokens will be frozen; users are advised to redeem or migrate their holdings promptly.  \
- Omni, the original USDT layer launched in 2014, is being phased out as Tether shifts focus to Layer 2 solutions like Bitcoin’s Lightning Network.  \
- Total USDT issued on these chains is relatively small: about $888M on Omni, $85M on EOS, $5M on Bitcoin Cash, $3.5M on Kusama, and $841K on Algorand.  \
- The move aligns with ongoing regulatory developments in the U.S. and EU, which are prompting stablecoin platform adjustments and delistings.  \
- USDT issuance remains concentrated on Tron and Ethereum, with over $155 billion in circulation, representing the majority of Tether’s $159 billion total supply.

news.headline: Fogo Launches Public Mainnet After Binance Token Sale
news.summary:
- Fogo, a Solana Virtual Machine-compatible Layer 1 blockchain focused on onchain trading, launched its public mainnet on Thursday, January 15, 2026.\
- The native FOGO token is trading around $0.053, experiencing a 12% drop, with over $208 million in 24-hour trading volume and a market cap near $198 million.\
- The mainnet launch followed a token sale via Binance that raised approximately $7 million by selling 2% of the supply at a $350 million valuation.\
- Fogo’s network boasts 40-millisecond block times and has demonstrated throughput exceeding 1,200 transactions per second with its initial live applications.\
- Over 10 decentralized applications (dApps), including Valiant (DEX) and Pyron (lending), are live at launch, with plans to double this count in the coming months.\
- The FOGO token is currently listed on centralized exchanges including Binance, OKX, Bybit, and Bitget.

news.headline: Morning Minute: Coinbase Withdraws Support of Clarity Act, Markup Delayed
news.summary:
- Coinbase publicly withdrew support for the Senate's Clarity Act crypto market structure legislation, citing concerns over tokenized equities bans and DeFi restrictions.\
- Following Coinbase’s withdrawal, the Senate Banking Committee delayed the markup vote for the Clarity Act.\
- Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated the updated bill version would be "materially worse than the current status quo," rather than accepting a suboptimal deal.\
- Key objections included potential erosion of CFTC authority, broad government access to DeFi data, and elimination of stablecoin rewards.\
- Bitcoin reached $96,750, as BTC ETFs saw $1.54B in net inflows over the past two sessions.\
- FUN and FOGO tokens both experienced their Token Generation Events (TGE) today, along with associated airdrops.

news.headline: SoSoValue hits $200 million valuation with $15 million Series A round
UK man who lost 8,000 bitcoin in landfill says he hasn’t given up, pivots to tokenizing legal claim
news.summary:
- James Howells, who lost 8,000 BTC in a landfill in 2013, has not given up on recovering his coins but has shifted his approach.  \
- He offered $33-40 million to buy the landfill site but has received no response from Newport authorities.  \
- Since excavation is no longer pursued, Howells plans to tokenize his legal ownership of the BTC via a new Layer 2 smart token called Ceiniog Coin (INI).  \
- The token, launching after October with an ICO later this year, aims to create a high-speed, Bitcoin-backed Web3 ecosystem.  \
- Howells maintains he remains the legal owner of the BTC, citing a January High Court ruling, despite the landfill ownership.  \
- His new strategy focuses on tokenizing his claim rather than landfill excavation, with the ultimate goal of asserting ownership of the 8,000 BTC.

news.headline: Crypto investors see 518% surge in gains in 2024, while ETH leads losses: CoinLedger
news.summary:
- Crypto investors saw a significant 518% surge in average portfolio gains in 2024, with an average gain of $5,482, up from $887.60 in 2023, according to CoinLedger.\
- SUPER, HYPE, and BTC were the top contributors to unrealized gains, while ETH, ADA, and POL led the losses, with ETH's decline attributed to competition from Solana.\
- CoinLedger's data, based on over 500,000 users primarily from the U.S., shows Bitcoin as the most held cryptocurrency, followed by ETH, XRP, and ADA.\
- Ledger was the most popular wallet for data imports, indicating a preference for hardware wallets, while Coinbase and Crypto.com surpassed Binance in exchange data imports due to regulatory challenges.\
- Despite regulatory issues, Binance.US remained a top-five exchange for imports, with Robinhood and KuCoin also popular among CoinLedger users.

news.headline: Battle Royale Shooter ‘OverTrip’ Hits Epic Games Store After Solana Token Launch
Nervos Network’s Force Bridge Cross-Chain Hack Exploits Access Control, Steals $3.9 Million in Ethereum and BNB
news.summary:
- Nervos Network’s Force Bridge, a cross-chain bridge between Ethereum and BNB, was hacked on June 3, 2025, stealing around $3.9 million in crypto.  \
- The breach was caused by an access control failure, according to blockchain security firm HashEx.  \
- A failed exploit attempt occurred roughly six hours before the successful attack.  \
- The incident underscores ongoing security vulnerabilities in cross-chain DeFi bridges.  \
- No details yet on whether the stolen funds have been recovered or if there are ongoing investigations.

news.headline: Hackers drain over $3 million in crypto from Nervos Network’s Force cross-chain bridge, say security analysts
news.summary:
- Hackers hijacked Nervos Network’s Force Bridge, stealing over $3 million in crypto assets.  \
- The stolen funds, including USDT, ETH, USDC, DAI, and wrapped BTC, were converted to ETH and sent through Tornado Cash.  \
- Magickbase, a key Nervos contributor, paused the bridge and is investigating; the protocol is set to sunset by November 2025.  \
- The attack highlights ongoing risks of DeFi cross-chain bridges, which have previously suffered major hacks like Ronin and Wormhole.  \
- Nervos launched its mainnet in 2019 to enable cross-chain interoperability, but low activity and high costs led to plans to sunset Force Bridge.  \
- The incident underscores the persistent security challenges in DeFi infrastructure.

news.headline: Non-custodial crypto wallet Safe reports fivefold revenue jump in 2025, not yet at ‘break-even’ profitability
news.summary:
- Non-custodial wallet Safe saw annualized revenue jump fivefold in 2025, reaching over $10 million from approximately $2 million in 2024.\
- The Safe Ecosystem Foundation confirmed the project is "not yet profitable" but aims to break even and double revenue in 2026.\
- Safe processed $600 billion in transaction volume in 2025, accounting for 43% of its total lifetime volume.\
- In 2025, users deployed 18.3 million new smart accounts on the platform, averaging one every 1.7 seconds.\
- Major entities utilizing Safe include the Ethereum Foundation, which migrated its 160,000 ETH treasury to the wallet, and Circle, which uses it for USDC storage.\
- The long-term goal for Safe is to achieve $100 million in annual recurring revenue by 2030.

news.headline: PSA: Don’t Keep Crypto at Home
Dogecoin Price Climbs as Shiba Inu Spikes to 8-Month High
news.summary:
- Shiba Inu (SHIB) surged to an eight-month high of $0.00002969 on Saturday before settling at $0.00002897, marking a 12% increase for the day.\
- SHIB is the top gainer among the top 50 cryptocurrencies by market cap, with Filecoin trailing behind with a 10% rise.\
- Dogecoin (DOGE) saw a more modest gain of 3%, reaching above $0.43, nearing a one-week high and up 164% over the past month.\
- Other dog-themed meme coins like Bonk (BONK) and Dogwifhat (WIF) saw minimal increases of around 1%, while Floki (FLOKI) rose by nearly 6%.\
- Brett (BRETT), a meme coin on Ethereum layer-2 network Base, hit an all-time high of $0.2032 and is up 17% in the past 24 hours.

news.headline: These Meme Coins Have Pumped More Than Dogecoin This Week
news.summary:
- Dogecoin (DOGE) recently hit a three-year high but has since dipped, ranking 57th in weekly price increase among meme coins with a 14.7% rise.\
- A Solana token inspired by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) surged 82.5%, outperforming the original DOGE.\
- Pepe (PEPE) saw a 65.8% increase, while Dogecoin's market cap nears $55 trillion, dwarfing Pepe's sub-$9 trillion cap.\
- Other meme coins like BRETT and BONK also outpaced DOGE, with 41.4% and 80.8% gains, respectively.\
- The biggest winners include ai16z (AI16Z) with a 693.9% jump, Comedian (BAN) up 356.5%, and PNUT rising 310%.\
- Low-liquidity tokens like Hasbulla’s Cat (BARSIK) can experience significant slippage, despite massive percentage gains, making it difficult to realize full profits.

news.headline: Coinbase to Suspend Trading of Floki, Turbo, Gigachad in New York on April 14, 2025 at 2 PM ET
news.summary:
- Coinbase will suspend trading of three meme coins—Floki (FLOKI), Turbo (TURBO), and Gigachad (GIGA)—in New York starting April 14, 2025, at 2 PM ET.\
- The decision follows a review of Coinbase's listed assets to ensure compliance with its listing standards.\
- Despite the announcement, the market response has been muted, with Floki gaining 3.6%, Giga rising 1.1%, and Turbo increasing by 5.4% in the last 24 hours.\
- The SEC has clarified that meme coins typically do not qualify as securities under U.S. law.\
- Legal experts suggest the suspension may be linked to a pending legal case in New York, reflecting Coinbase's efforts to adapt to regulatory and market changes.

news.headline: Coinbase Suspends Memecoins in NY as Prices Tank
news.summary:
- Coinbase has suspended trading of certain memecoins, including GIGA, TURBO, and FLOKI, for New York users, effective April 14, 2025.\
- The decision comes less than two months after these tokens were listed, with GIGA and TURBO initially added to Coinbase's listing roadmap in December 2024.\
- Since their listing, GIGA's market cap has dropped 82% to $120 million, and TURBO's has fallen 63% to $145 million. FLOKI's valuation has also decreased by 56% to $570 million.\
- The specific reasons for the delisting were not disclosed, but the move aligns with a broader trend of exchanges like ByBit also pruning their token offerings.\
- Despite these actions, Robinhood is expanding its memecoin listings, adding tokens like POPCAT, PNUT, and PENGU, which have seen significant declines from their all-time highs.

news.headline: Lido’s new stVaults will let L2s create their own rules for Ethereum staking
news.summary:
- Lido Labs Foundation launched stVaults on the Ethereum mainnet Friday, enabling external builders to use Lido’s staking infrastructure.\
- stVaults create isolated staking environments allowing teams to customize validator configurations while accessing Lido’s existing liquidity integrations.\
- This signals a shift from Lido's single-product model toward shared, customizable staking solutions for Layer-2s and specialized setups.\
- Consensys's Layer-2 network, Linea, is an initial deployment partner, using stVaults to manage bridged ETH rewards for ecosystem incentives.\
- Nansen is also utilizing stVaults to launch its first Ethereum staking product.\
- The core Lido staking protocol remains operational alongside the new stVault framework.

news.headline: Lido rolls out stVaults on mainnet with Day 1 users including Linea, Nansen and several institutional stakers
'Forgotten Runiverse' on Ethereum Network Ronin Is the Latest Crypto Gaming Casualty
news.summary:
- Forgotten Runiverse, an MMORPG on the Ronin network, is taking its game servers offline until further notice due to financial infeasibility.\
- The team behind the game, linked to the Forgotten Runes NFT collection, stated that maintaining the game live in its current state is no longer financially viable.\
- All account and progress data will be preserved securely, though the free-to-play, browser-based game servers will shut down on Tuesday, January 28, 2026.\
- This closure reflects a broader trend in the crypto gaming sector, following the shutdowns of titles like Deadrop, Nyan Heroes, and Pirate Nation last year.\
- Forgotten Runiverse had previously secured funding from investors including Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian and had approval for development across Nintendo, PlayStation, and Xbox.\
- The developers maintain the project is not abandoned, referring to the pause as the "Runiverse isn’t gone, merely resting."

news.headline: Movement Pivots to Layer 1 as On-Chain Activity Declines
news.summary:
- Movement, an Ethereum L2, plans to launch its own Layer 1 to boost performance to over 10,000 TPS with native staking and validator security, amid declining on-chain activity.  \
- The network’s monthly transactions have fallen 34% from 7.3 million in June to 4.8 million in August, reflecting a broader decline in usage since late 2024.  \
- Since debuting as an Ethereum L2 in late 2024, Movement has processed 26.7 million transactions, ranking above Celestia but below peaq and Polkadot.  \
- The transition to L1 aims to eliminate centralized sequencers and enable seamless migration of network state and data, though the timeline remains unclear.  \
- The move follows controversy involving co-founder Rushi Manche, who sued the project after suspension and termination over market-making issues; MOVE token has dropped over 90% from its December 2024 high.  \
- Last month, Ronin pivoted back to Ethereum as an L2 amid declining activity and a 95% drop in total value locked from its peak.

news.headline: Flow exploit leaves NFT loan borrowers ‘in limbo’ after network pause
news.summary:
- An exploit on the Flow blockchain has resulted in NFT loan borrowers being left in an uncertain situation.\
- The incident forced an immediate pause of the Flow network.\
- The exploit specifically impacted users engaged in NFT lending protocols on the network.\
- The network pause, enacted following the exploit, is currently ongoing as of December 31, 2025.\
- Borrowers are currently unable to manage or resolve their outstanding NFT loans.

news.headline: Flow Back Online After Token Drops 40% on $4M Exploit
news.summary:
- The Layer 1 blockchain Flow experienced an exploit on December 27, causing over 24 hours of downtime and the network to resume operations earlier today, December 29, 2025.\
- The attack resulted in the transfer of nearly $4 million in crypto assets from Flow's execution layer, though the Flow Foundation stated user balances were unaffected.\
- The FLOW token price plummeted by 40% over the weekend, dropping its market capitalization from $280 million to $170 million early Saturday morning.\
- Flow initially planned a chain rollback to revert the stolen funds but pivoted to an alternative solution after pushback, which was later praised by third parties like deBridge.\
- Leading bridge solutions, including Stargate Finance and deBridge, confirmed they will resume activity on Flow once the network is fully restored.\
- Dapper Labs characterized the incident as a "stress test" for the ecosystem and stated a detailed post-mortem report will be published soon.

news.headline: Crypto Markets See Moderate Gains as US Inflation Hits 3%
Bitcoin Attempts to Reclaim $110,000 as US Stocks Rebound
news.summary:
- Bitcoin and Ether rose about 1.5% on October 23, with Bitcoin holding around $110,000 as US stocks and Treasury yields climbed; market cap is $3.78 trillion.  \
- Bitcoin’s recent gains are seen as consolidating within $108K-$112K, but macro volatility and risk aversion could limit upside, according to analysts.  \
- Long-term Bitcoin holders have sold over 28,000 BTC ($3 billion) in the past week, despite the psychological $100K level.  \
- Top gainers include ChainOpera AI (+39%) and Kinetiq Staked (+9.8%), while Zcash and Hedera declined over 8%.  \
- $345 million in leveraged crypto positions were liquidated in 24 hours, with Ethereum accounting for over $108 million of the total.  \
- Spot Ethereum ETFs experienced over $18.7 million in outflows, and Bitcoin ETFs saw more than $101 million in net outflows; US Treasury yields rose amid macro concerns.

news.headline: Espresso Token Launches at $275 Million Valuation
news.summary:
- Espresso, a decentralized rollup base layer, launched its native ESP token this morning at a ~$275 million valuation.\
- The token debuted at $0.072, climbing to $0.083 shortly after, with $115 million in trading volume recorded in the first 7 hours.\
- The launch facilitates the network's transition to a proof-of-stake consensus mechanism.\
- 10% of the total token supply was distributed via an airdrop to over one million eligible addresses today.\
- Investors from the July 2025 Kaito Launchpad sale, who bought 1% of the supply at a $400 million valuation, are down 31% with a 2-year vesting period remaining.\
- ESP joins other recent token launches like Infinex and Aztec that are opening underwater for initial sale participants.

news.headline: Kaito and Polymarket Unveil 'Attention Markets'
news.summary:
- Kaito and Polymarket launched 'Attention Markets,' combining attention measurement tech with prediction market infrastructure.\
- The new markets are designed to track the dynamics of cultural narratives and emerging trends.\
- Prediction market trading volume surged 850% year-over-year, hitting $6.2 billion in weekly volume as of January 2026.\
- Kaito CEO Yu Hu stated attention is the scarce resource in an information-abundant world, positioning these as a new market category.\
- Attention Markets will cover verticals including AI, finance, and entertainment.\
- The markets will be accessible on both the Polymarket and Kaito platforms.

news.headline: Olas raises $13.8 million to launch Pearl, an ‘app store’ for autonomous AI agents in crypto
news.summary:
- Olas, also known as Autonolas, has secured $13.8 million in funding to launch Pearl, an app store for autonomous AI agents in the crypto space.\
- The funding round was led by 1kx, with participation from Tioga Capital, Sigil Fund, Zee Prime Capital, Borderless, Keyrock, and others.\
- Pearl aims to allow users to own and control AI agents on-chain for various applications, including autonomous social media accounts and DeFi trading.\
- Olas introduced the Olas Accelerator program, offering up to $1 million in grants and OLAS token rewards to developers building on the platform.\
- The Olas technology stack, including the Olas Stack and Protocol, supports the creation and co-ownership of AI agents, having facilitated over 3.5 million transactions across nine blockchains.\
- Use cases for these transactions include DeFAI through Modius, social influencer agents via Agents.Fun, and prediction markets with Olas Predict.

news.headline: 4 predictions for privacy in 2026
news.summary:
- Privacy in 2026 will pivot from absolute privacy/transparency to practical tradeoffs, potentially offering conditional privacy for high-risk transactions.\
- Private stablecoins are predicted to become a core layer of onchain payment infrastructure, integrating configurable privacy and policy controls for compliance.\
- Privacy solutions like Aztec, Nightfall, and Railgun are expected to move from testnet to production, marking the industrialization of onchain privacy technology.\
- The focus will shift to "threat-resistant" onchain privacy, discouraging malicious actors while providing pragmatic solutions for businesses and individuals.\
- Threat-resistant solutions are categorized as either throttled privacy (with delays) or responsible privacy (with an information custodian for tracing).\
- A key challenge in 2026 will be the lack of consumer-facing wallet support for new privacy capabilities and inconsistent regulatory approaches.

news.headline: Jill Gunter has wallet drained via vulnerable ThirdWeb contract
news.summary:
- Jill Gunter, co-founder of Espresso, reported her wallet was drained of funds due to a vulnerability in a ThirdWeb bridge contract she had previously used.\
- The theft, which occurred on December 9, 2025, resulted in the loss of over $30,000 USDC, which was funneled into the privacy protocol Railgun.\
- ThirdWeb confirmed the legacy contract vulnerability was discovered in April 2025, allowing access to funds from users who accepted unlimited token approvals.\
- ThirdWeb stated the vulnerability resulted from the legacy contract not being properly decommissioned and confirmed the contract has since been permanently disabled.\
- Gunter noted the "deep irony" of the theft as she was preparing a defense of crypto privacy to present in Washington D.C. next week.\
- Gunter pledged to donate any recovered funds to the SEAL Security Alliance and criticized a previous ThirdWeb security disclosure from late 2023 as potentially giving hackers a "head start."

news.headline: Securitize, VanEck Bring VBILL Tokenized Treasury Fund To Aave
news.summary:
- Securitize and VanEck have launched the VBILL tokenized treasury fund on Aave, enabling DeFi access to traditional US Treasury assets.  \
- The fund tokenizes US Treasury bills, offering a new bridge between traditional finance and crypto markets.  \
- The initiative aims to provide institutional investors with more efficient, transparent access to treasury yields via DeFi platforms.  \
- As of now, Bitcoin trades around $102,885, with other major cryptocurrencies showing modest gains; market remains stable.  \
- The broader crypto market continues to see cautious optimism amid ongoing regulatory developments and institutional interest.

news.headline: RWA giant Securitize eyeing public listing via Cantor Fitzgerald SPAC at unicorn valuation: Bloomberg
news.summary:
- Securitize, a leading RWA tokenization firm with over $4.6 billion in assets, is in talks to go public via a merger with Cantor Fitzgerald’s SPAC, CEPT, at a valuation over $1 billion.  \
- The deal is still under discussion, and Securitize may choose to remain private; no final decision has been announced.  \
- CEPT, which raised $240 million in its IPO, saw its shares jump over 12.5% after Bloomberg’s report.  \
- Securitize has backing from major firms like BlackRock, Morgan Stanley, Coinbase Ventures, and Jump Crypto, and manages the BlackRock BUIDL fund, tokenizing over $2.8 billion in U.S. Treasuries.  \
- The firm recently launched offramps for BUIDL and VanEck’s VBILL funds using Ripple’s RLUSD stablecoin.  \
- As of October 2025, the potential IPO would mark a significant milestone for the RWA sector, but no final decision has been made.

news.headline: Euro Stablecoin Market Cap Doubles in Year After MiCA, Study Finds
news.summary:
- Euro-denominated stablecoin market cap more than doubled in the 12 months following the June 2024 implementation of MiCA regulations, recovering from a 48% decline in the previous year.\
- This growth contrasts with a 26% increase in the total stablecoin market capitalization over the same period.\
- By May 2025, the euro stablecoin market cap reached about $500 million, and data tracked by CoinGecko places it at $680 million as of the report.\
- Tokens EURS, EURC, and EURCV showed the most significant gains, with EURS soaring 644% to $283.9 million by October 2025.\
- Monthly euro stablecoin transaction volume increased nearly ninefold after MiCA, jumping to US$3.83 billion.\
- Consumer search interest for euro stablecoins saw substantial spikes across several EU countries, including 400% growth in Finland and 313.3% in Italy.

news.headline: Société Générale taps Uniswap and Morpho in DeFi lending push
news.summary:
- Société Générale’s digital assets arm, SG Forge, integrated euro and dollar stablecoins with Uniswap and Morpho, enabling institutional clients to swap, lend, and borrow crypto assets.  \
- This marks one of the first major banks moving beyond pilots to actively utilize DeFi infrastructure amid rising crypto lending, which hit a record $130 billion in deposits in early September.  \
- Major DeFi protocols like Aave, Morpho, and Euler are attracting significant liquidity and generating substantial transaction fee earnings.  \
- Société Générale has been exploring DeFi since 2021, issuing regulated USDCV and EURCV stablecoins on Ethereum and Solana in 2023.  \
- Broader regulatory developments, including the EU’s MiCA rules and US stablecoin legislation, are increasing banks’ confidence to engage with digital assets and DeFi.  \
- Future US regulation, such as the market structure bill, could further accelerate bank adoption of DeFi, potentially setting a precedent for more traditional finance institutions.

news.headline: Binance token listing no longer a ‘bullish’ event, research
news.summary:
- Over 94% of tokens listed on Binance in 2024 and 2025 are currently in the red, with losses ranging from 17% to 97%, according to research by crypto analyst Dethective.\
- In 2025, the 27 tokens listed by Binance had an average loss of 44%, with only three tokens yielding positive returns and 13 experiencing losses over 50%.\
- The research suggests that Binance's token listings, once seen as a mark of legitimacy, are now viewed as "pure exit liquidity," potentially damaging the perception of crypto among newcomers.\
- In 2024, nearly 98% of tokens listed on Binance were in the red, with an average loss of 54.6%; only one token, BABYDOGE, showed a positive return of 8.9%.\
- Despite a generally bearish market in 2025, 2024 was considered bullish, yet most tokens still depreciated after listing on Binance.\
- Binance has introduced a "Vote to List" initiative, allowing the public to decide which crypto projects should be listed, following criticism of its listing process by former CEO Changpeng Zhao.

news.headline: Baby Doge and Neiro Meme Coins Surge as Bitcoin DOG Token Hits All-Time High
news.summary:
- Dog-themed meme coins, including DOG•GO•TO•THE•MOON (DOG), Baby Doge Coin (BABYDOGE), and Neiro (NEIRO), have surged in value recently.\
- DOG, a Bitcoin-based meme coin, hit an all-time high of $0.0096 with a market cap of $966 million, despite not being listed on a tier-1 exchange.\
- Baby Doge Coin on BNB Chain saw a 33% price increase, nearing its previous peak, while Neiro on Ethereum rose 10%, reaching a $1.1 billion market cap last month.\
- DOG, Neiro, and Baby Doge Coin experienced significant trading volumes on Gate.io, with $61 million, $56 million, and $187 million respectively.\
- Runes protocol, developed by Casey Rodarmor, continues to influence Bitcoin transactions, accounting for 8% of network activity on Sunday.

news.headline: Degens Are Flocking to These Solana Meme Coins Amid Crypto Market Turmoil
news.summary:
- Despite a turbulent crypto market, traders are still profiting from Solana meme coins, with some coins defying the overall negative trend.\
- Meme coins have seen significant declines, with Ethereum layer-2 Base meme coins down 7.3% and Solana meme coins down 4.4% over the past week.\
- The market sentiment is low, but traders are capitalizing on low-cap meme coins, with some tokens reaching multi-million dollar market caps.\
- Notable meme coins like Rope (ROPE) and McValue (MCVALUE) have seen significant gains, though they remain highly volatile and risky.\
- Fartcoin (FARTCOIN) and AI Rig Complex (ARC) are among the few meme coins in the green, with ARC, a Solana-based AI meme coin, climbing 43%.\
- Traders are advised to consider coins with strong fundamentals, as meme coins often result in losses for the majority of investors.

news.headline: $$600B gone in 30 minutes — inside crypto’s fastest-ever flash crash
news.summary:
- Crypto markets lost over $600 billion in market cap within 30 minutes on October 10, following Donald Trump’s tariff war escalation with China.  \
- Major assets like Cosmos, Ravencoin, Polkadot, Dogecoin, Cardano, and DigiByte experienced 55-99.9% flash crashes during the panic.  \
- Binance paid $283 million in compensation due to liquidations of locally-depegged assets amid the sell-off.  \
- A leveraged trader reportedly made over $190 million by shorting crypto during the crash, raising suspicions of advance knowledge.  \
- The cascade of liquidations was fueled by cross-margin collateral, causing smaller coins to liquidate larger ones, with 1.6 million accounts affected.  \
- The crash was driven by panic selling, safe-haven flows into gold, and the rapid unwinding of thinly traded and leveraged positions.

news.headline: CoinDesk Market Index Adds Convex, Serum, 12 Other Digital Assets
deBridge launches Reserve Fund to buy back its DBR token with all protocol revenue
news.summary:
- deBridge Foundation launched a Reserve Fund that uses 100% of protocol revenue to buy back its DBR token on the open market.  \
- Since June, the fund has acquired 1.3% of DBR’s 10 billion supply, worth nearly $3 million.  \
- The broader treasury holds $30.1 million across DBR, USDC, SOL, and ETH, with plans to explore yield strategies like Aave, Lido, and Kamino Finance.  \
- The initiative aims to boost long-term value and transparency, with a public dashboard tracking purchases, yields, and balances.  \
- deBridge, a cross-chain liquidity protocol launched in 2022, has nearly $10 million in annualized fees and a market cap over $80 million for its DBR token.  \
- The buyback approach aligns with recent moves by platforms like dYdX and Raydium to support ecosystem growth through revenue-based token buybacks.

news.headline: deBridge Token Goes Live With DBR Airdropped to 491K Wallets
news.summary:
- deBridge's governance token, DBR, launched with an airdrop to 491,286 early users and community members.\
- DBR, an SPL token on Solana, has an initial circulation of 1.8 billion out of a maximum 10 billion supply.\
- Recipients can claim their tokens in two phases: 50% at the token generation event (TGE) and 50% six months later, or 80% at TGE with a 20% penalty.\
- Trading for DBR began at $0.03 per token, targeting a fully diluted valuation of $300 million.\
- DBR will enable holders to vote on future strategies and implementations of the deBridge protocol, with potential staking features planned.\
- deBridge has facilitated over $3.3 billion in bridged volume between Solana and Ethereum since April 2023, generating significant revenue.

news.headline: Privacy-preserving USAD stablecoin launches on Aleo Layer 1 mainnet via Paxos partnership
news.summary:
- Paxos Labs is issuing a privacy-preserving stablecoin, USAD, set to launch natively on the ZK-powered Aleo Layer 1 mainnet.\
- The USAD stablecoin is designed to support traditional payment use cases alongside programmable scenarios that are difficult on fully transparent blockchains.\
- Aleo utilizes zero-knowledge cryptography to ensure that sensitive details like participant identities and transaction amounts are hidden from public view while maintaining auditability.\
- USAD will be backed 1:1 by cash and cash equivalents managed by Paxos, which also issues stablecoins for clients like PayPal.\
- This move follows a prior announcement that rival stablecoin issuer Circle is also piloting a privacy-preserving version of USDC, called USDCx, on Aleo.\
- Paxos co-founder Bhau Kotecha stated the goal is to bring "digital dollars into an environment where privacy and programmability are built in from the start" for enterprises.

news.headline: From Aztec to Zcash: The year ‘pragmatic privacy’ took root
Your Favorite TradFi Assets Meet Crypto Perps
news.summary:
- Hyperliquid is emerging as a leading venue for perpetual contracts based on traditional finance assets, using its permissionless HIP-3 framework.\
- Builders have deployed custom perpetual markets on Hyperliquid that offer leveraged exposure to assets like Tesla stock, gold, and the S&P 500.\
- Three main providers for traditional asset perps on Hyperliquid are Trade (Unit), Felix, and Kinetiq, each with a different approach to margin and incentives.\
- Trade (from Unit) currently offers the deepest liquidity for USDC-margined perps across stocks, indices, and commodities.\
- Felix specializes in USDH-margined perps for commodities and equities and explicitly offers trading points that are expected to be considered for a future token launch.\
- Kinetiq's Markets focuses on USDH-margined index perps and runs an ongoing kPoints program to reward trading volume.

news.headline: Controversial Hyperliquid HIP-5 Proposal Leaves Community Split
news.summary:
- Hyperliquid is considering HIP-5, a proposal to create a second assistance fund (AF2) using up to 5% of protocol fees to support ecosystem tokens beyond HYPE.  \
- The current system allocates 99% of revenue to buy back HYPE tokens; HIP-5 would diversify support to tokens like PURR, Kinetiq, Felix, and others.  \
- HYPE stakers would vote on which ecosystem tokens to buy and in what amounts, aiming to incentivize builders and governance participation.  \
- The proposal has split the community: some fear it could lead to bribery, misuse, or exit liquidity issues, citing past problems with Cosmos $ATOM and Polkadot $DOT.  \
- Supporters argue HIP-5 would boost ecosystem growth, utility for $HYPE, and encourage more development on Hyperliquid.  \
- The debate highlights ongoing tensions between decentralization concerns and ecosystem incentivization in DeFi governance.

news.headline: Ledger: The Latest Nano and New Hard Wallet Features
Interest in Ether Derivatives Surges Ahead of Merge
AI Token Frenzy Continues
news.summary:
- The AI token craze on Solana is intensifying, driven by the rapid price increases of tokens like AI16z and VIRTUAL.\
- Traders are flocking to new AI-related tokens, hoping to capitalize on the next big surge.\
- Over the past 48 hours, mid-cap AI tokens such as SWARMS, Universal Operating System (UOS), and Alchemist (ALCH) have reached new all-time highs.\
- SWARMS has increased by 140% to a $175 million valuation, ALCH has doubled to $218 million, and UOS has surged 525% to $26 million since January 1.\
- The frenzy has also led to a spike in activity on platforms like PumpFun, with daily active users nearing all-time highs.\
- Despite the hype, some investors believe that the leading AI tokens will be those that first gained traction, regardless of their user interface or experience.

news.headline: There Is Too Much Friction in Web3 For Newcomers. Here’s How We Fix it.
news.summary:
- Web3 onboarding is hampered by complex network switching, causing 80% of crypto users to quit within 90 days, due to poor user experience.  \
- The "Networks" dropdown in wallets like MetaMask highlights industry focus on expansion over simplicity, making users think about chains instead of applications.  \
- Cross-chain interactions are cumbersome, involving bridges, multiple approvals, gas fees, and tracking across explorers, deterring mainstream adoption.  \
