- The initiative is part of Frax’s Frax Universal Interface (FUI) and its vision to become the decentralized central bank of crypto.\
- Other roadmap updates include rebranding the FRAX stablecoin and a network upgrade via a hard fork.\
- Frax’s Layer 2 blockchain, Fraxtal, introduced last year, uses decentralized sequencers and rewards users for interacting with smart contracts.

news.headline: Associated Press to Launch NFT Marketplace on Polygon to Support Journalism
Wallet tied to $200M Mixin Network hack moves ETH after over two years of dormancy: onchain data
news.summary:
- A wallet tied to the September 2023 Mixin Network exploit moved 2,005 ETH (approx. $3.85 million) to the privacy mixer Tornado Cash after nearly two years of dormancy.\
- Three new wallets received a combined 2,087 ETH from Tornado Cash shortly after the transfer and sold the funds at about $1,933 per ETH.\
- The wallet linked to the breach still holds substantial assets: 57,849 ETH (approx. $113.4 million) and 891 BTC (approx. $59.7 million).\
- The original exploit in 2023 resulted in the loss of approximately $200 million, comprising 59,854 ETH, 891 BTC, and about $23.57 million in USDT, which was swapped for DAI.\
- Following the breach, Mixin temporarily suspended deposit and withdrawal services and offered a $20 million bounty to the hacker.\
- This activity follows a pattern where attackers often leave stolen funds untouched for extended periods before attempting to launder them through obfuscation tools.

news.headline: Tornado Cash Developer Roman Semenov Added to FBI's 'Most Wanted' List
news.summary:
- Tornado Cash developer Roman Semenov has been added to the FBI's "Most Wanted" list after being on the run for nearly 30 months.\
- An arrest warrant was issued for Semenov on August 21, 2023.\
- The federal charges against him include Conspiracy to Commit Money Laundering, Conspiracy to Operate an Unlicensed Money Transmitting Business, and Conspiracy to Violate the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.\
- The FBI notes Semenov has ties to Turkey, Dubai (United Arab Emirates), and Moscow, Russia.\
- Fellow Tornado Cash defendant Roman Storm was convicted last August of Conspiring to Operate an Unlicensed Money Transmitting Business.\
- Storm's jury deadlocked on the two more serious charges against him.

news.headline: The Protocol: Monad Airdrop + Blockchain Go Live
SoftBank-backed Improbable’s Layer 1 Somnia launches mainnet and SOMI token
news.summary:
- SoftBank-backed Improbable launched the mainnet for its Layer 1 blockchain Somnia and its SOMI token today.  \
- Somnia is designed for commercial-scale applications like DeFi, social, and gaming, after a six-month testnet processing over 10 billion transactions.  \
- The ecosystem has onboarded more than 118 million wallets, with over 70 active projects including Variance and Maelstrom.  \
- The mainnet delivers over 1 million transactions per second with sub-second finality, marking a significant step for real-world blockchain use cases.  \
- Improbable sold its gaming services subsidiary MPG in 2023 to focus on metaverse and blockchain development, collaborating with Yuga Labs on Otherside.  \
- The Somnia ecosystem has attracted $270 million in combined capital to date.

news.headline: Somnia Mainnet Goes Live Along With Native SOMI Token After 10B Testnet Transactions
news.summary:
- Somnia mainnet launched today, debuting its native SOMI token after successfully completing 10 billion testnet transactions.  \
- The mainnet launch marks a significant milestone, demonstrating robust network activity and readiness for mainnet operations.  \
- SOMI token is now live, enabling users to participate in the ecosystem and access new features.  \
- The project emphasizes scalability and security, with the testnet transactions highlighting strong network performance.  \
- No specific market impact or price movement details provided in the article.  \
- The launch aligns with broader industry trends of expanding blockchain infrastructure and token utility.

news.headline: Big Time Studios Sets December 2 as the Launch of the New PvP Mode for 2024’s Most Successful NFT Video Game - Big Time
news.summary:
- Big Time Studios has announced the launch of a new PvP mode for their successful NFT video game, Big Time.\
- The new mode is set to be released on December 2.\
- Big Time is recognized as one of the most successful NFT video games of 2024.\
- The company uses cookies to enhance user experience and share data with social media, advertising, and analytics partners.

news.headline: Big Time Generates over $100M in Revenue since Preseason
news.summary:
- Big Time Studios' free-to-play multiplayer action/MMO RPG, Big Time, has generated over $100M in revenue.\
- Players transacted a total volume of over $230M without selling any tokens.\
- The game combines the gameplay of action RPGs like Diablo with the realms of MMOs like World of Warcraft.\
- Players collect essential resources for crafting valuable virtual goods represented by NFTs.\
- The game's unique approach blends gaming excitement with the world of digital collectibles.\
- Big Time Studios prioritizes the player community by preventing non-players from purchasing their way to success.

news.headline: Spanish bank Bankinter joins BBVA and Tether with stake in crypto exchange Bit2Me
news.summary:
- Spanish bank Bankinter has acquired a minority stake in crypto exchange Bit2Me, joining a €30 million ($33 million) funding round previously involving Tether.\
- The investment is intended to strengthen Bit2Me's capital structure and support its regulatory objectives across Europe and Latin America.\
- Bit2Me operates under the EU's new MiCA regulation, positioning itself as a B2B gateway for banks seeking compliant crypto access.\
- This deal adds Bankinter to Bit2Me's existing traditional financial institution backers, which include BBVA, Unicaja, and Cecabank.\
- Bit2Me CFO Pablo Casadío stated the alliance confirms banks are choosing collaboration over competition to leverage Bit2Me's industry know-how.\
- Spanish bank Unicaja, via its arm Unicaja Ventures, previously acquired a stake of over 5% in Bit2Me in July 2024.

news.headline: The number of BTC wallets holding more than 0.1BTC hasn’t grown in two years
news.summary:
- The number of unique Bitcoin addresses holding over 0.1 BTC has declined over the past 24 months, falling from 4,548,107 on December 8, 2023, to 4,443,541 today.\
- This 2.3% decline marks the first two-year period in Bitcoin's history where this specific metric has decreased.\
- The downturn suggests investors are increasingly unwilling to keep large BTC balances concentrated in single wallets.\
- This trend may be due to the proliferation of BTC proxies like ETFs, or advanced security practices like unspent transaction output consolidation and key distribution.\
- The article also details Tether's extensive investment portfolio, noting CEO Paolo Ardoino claims investments in over 120 companies across crypto, AI, energy, and media.\
- Identified Tether investments range from BTC mining projects (Volcano Energy, Bitdeer) to financial services (Ledn, Bit2Me) and controversial entities like the now-defunct Celsius.

news.headline: Love them or hate them, memecoins are winning either way
news.summary:
- **Memecoins Surge**: Memecoins have gained significant traction, now making up over 10% of the top 250 cryptocurrencies, with several reaching billion-dollar market caps quickly.\
- **Rapid Growth**: Seven memecoins launched since 2022, including BONK, BOME, and PEPE, have hit the $1 billion mark, with BOME achieving this in just four days.\
- **Market Dynamics**: The SEC's regulatory actions may be driving interest in memecoins, as traders seek alternatives to traditional investment contracts.\
- **Election Impact**: Jason Yanowitz of Blockworks suggests that a new U.S. administration, regardless of party, could be beneficial for crypto, with potential for Bitcoin to hit all-time highs by year-end.\
- **VC Activity**: Venture capital investment in crypto saw a 20% decrease last quarter, with 2024 expected to slightly surpass 2023 in funding, indicating a cautious but ongoing interest in the sector.

news.headline: Solana daily transaction fees drop to multi-month low as memecoin hype cools
Liquity Protocol Suffers $30 Million of Outflows After Disclosing V2 Bug
news.summary:
- Liquity Protocol, an Ethereum-based lending platform, experienced approximately $30 million in outflows over two days following the disclosure of a bug in its V2 Stability Pools.\
- On February 12, Liquity advised users to close their Earn positions as a precaution while investigating a potential issue with the V2 Stability Pools.\
- A follow-up announcement on February 13 confirmed the issue with the Stability Pools, though no further details on the cause were provided.\
- Liquity assured users that the problem was isolated to the Stability Pools and that no users had been impacted.\
- The protocol plans to redeploy V2 with a patch and will update users through official channels.\
- Liquity V2 was launched on January 23, and its LUSD stablecoin currently has a market capitalization of $57 million.

news.headline: Liquity Embraces NFTs in V2 Stablecoin Upgrade
The Daily: Robinhood takes aim at larger prediction-market share, Grayscale files to launch first-ever Zcash ETF, and more
On-Chain Games Don’t Have a ‘Secret Sauce,’ YGG’s New Advisor Says
news.summary:
- Crypto gaming remains hit-driven, with success largely based on volume and iteration rather than a "secret sauce," says Quinn Campbell, new advisor to YGG Play.  \
- Campbell emphasizes the importance of testing numerous game prototypes at scale, learning from failures, rather than relying on early hype or predictions.  \
- YGG is adopting a systematic approach to publishing, focusing on scalable monetization models and diverse game formats to find what works.  \
- The target audience is "Casual Degens" — crypto-native players interested in fun, rewarding, and quick gameplay loops, often overlooked by traditional studios.  \
- Campbell’s experience highlights the need for repeated testing and data-driven decision-making to achieve sustainable success in on-chain gaming.  \
- YGG aims to develop a repeatable, scalable model that aligns with crypto players’ habits, moving beyond one-off prototypes to long-term growth.

news.headline: ‘Ragnarok: Landverse’ Expanding to Ethereum Gaming Network Ronin
VCs Show 'Flight to Quality' in Q2 Funding Report
news.summary:
- Venture capitalists showed a "flight to quality" in Q2, with fewer but larger crypto deals, according to PitchBook's latest report.\
- Crypto fundraising rose 2.5% to $2.7 billion across 503 deals in Q1, despite a 12.5% drop in the number of deals.\
- Major Q2 deals included Monad ($225M Series A), Berachain ($100M Series B), Babylon ($70M early-stage), Farcaster ($150M Series A), and Zentry ($140M early-stage).\
- Infrastructure, including scaling and financial services, saw the highest volume of fundraising.\
- PitchBook's Robert Le forecasts a 20%+ increase in overall fundraising for 2024, predicting $12-$14 billion compared to $10 billion last year.\
- Le anticipates consolidation in blockchain networks, with Solana, Bitcoin, Optimism, Arbitrum, and Base emerging as key players, while many projects may become "zombie chains."\
- Decentralized physical infrastructure (DePIN) is highlighted as a potential standout sector, appealing to non-native crypto users.

news.headline: Chiliz Fan Tokens Surge In Spite Of Weak Crypto Market Action
news.summary:
- Chiliz fan tokens are experiencing a significant surge, defying the broader downtrend in the digital asset market.\
- The top 10 fan tokens by market cap have all posted double-digit gains over the past week, with OG, the token of the European Esports group, leading with a 163.8% increase.\
- Paris Saint-Germain's PSG token saw a 47.7% rise, followed by S.S. Lazio (33%), Alpine F1 team (29.6%), and Santos FC (29.2%).\
- On October 7, fan tokens drove over $1 billion in daily volume for the first time, with OG accounting for $563 million of that volume.\
- The combined market cap of fan tokens stands at $322.7 million, with Chiliz's native token CHZ up 15.4% over the past week.\
- This surge comes as the overall cryptocurrency market remains relatively stagnant, with a slight 0.36% increase in the combined market cap over the past seven days.

news.headline: Fan tokens’ trade volume surpasses $1 billion in 24 hours, OG Esports leads surge
news.summary:
- Fan crypto tokens' 24-hour trading volume surpassed $1 billion, with OG Esports' fan token leading the surge.\
- Data from CoinMarketCap shows a 219.98% increase in fan token trade volume, reaching $1.08 billion, and a 15.94% rise in market capitalization to $348.5 million.\
- OG Fan Token, linked to European Esports group OG, saw over 51 million tokens traded, totaling around $563 million.\
- The OG-USDT pair on Binance alone accounted for $282.33 million in trades, surpassing the volume of major tokens like Toncoin and Cardano.\
- OG’s price surged 87% in the last 24 hours to $10.50, with a 245% increase over the past week; other fan tokens like Santos FC and Alpine F1 also saw significant price growth.\
- Fan tokens enable sports fans to engage in team decisions and access exclusive benefits, such as voting on merchandise and participating in special events.

news.headline: Former 'bitcoin mayor' Eric Adams denies profiting from NYC Token after 80% crash
news.summary:
- Eric Adams’ team vehemently denies that the former NYC mayor profited from or moved any funds related to the NYC Token following a sharp liquidity withdrawal.\
- On-chain data showed a $3 million USDC liquidity removal from the Solana-based token near its peak, causing an immediate 80% price collapse.\
- Approximately $1.5 million of the withdrawn liquidity was later returned to the pool, while roughly $900,000 remained unaccounted for, sparking rug pull accusations.\
- The NYC Token, which Adams promoted for civic causes like education scholarships, is currently trading around $0.13, having lost over $400 million in market value from its high.\
- The token team claimed the initial move was a "rebalance" due to high demand, but the controversy has increased scrutiny on politically branded tokens.

news.headline: Former NYC Mayor Eric Adams disputes memecoin rug pull allegations, claims he made no profit
Morning Minute: Is Crypto's 4-Year Cycle Over?
news.summary:
- Arthur Hayes declares the 4-year Bitcoin cycle over, attributing market moves to liquidity cycles rather than halving events, with expectations of shallower, liquidity-driven pullbacks in 2025.  \
- Hayes and others highlight bullish macro trends: easier monetary policy, increased demand for spot ETFs, and policy shifts in the US and China supporting Bitcoin’s rise.  \
- Major cryptocurrencies are slightly down; Bitcoin at $121,500 (-1%), ETH at $4,340 (-1%), while ZEC (+19%) and LTC (+12%) are top movers on strong support from Grayscale and Naval.  \
- Bitcoin ETF inflows continue, with $198M added yesterday; ETH ETF flows broke streak, and Luxembourg’s sovereign wealth fund allocated ~$8M into BTC ETFs.  \
- Notable memecoin movements are mixed; Dogecoin +1%, Shiba -1%, and meme traders face losses, with 90% of Sol-to-BNB bridge traders losing money.  \
- NFT market mostly red; Doodles (+6%) and a Beeple sale ($200,600) stand out amid overall declines.

news.headline: Morning Minute: Bitcoin vs Gold and the Race to New ATHs
Kujira Foundation's Tokens Stung by Its Own Leveraged Positions as Bets Backfire
Kujira Token Drops 40% After the Foundation Gets Liquidated
news.summary:
- Kujira’s native token KUJI plummeted 42% after the foundation was liquidated on its leveraged liquidity positions.\
- The chain's total value locked (TVL) dropped 22% to $38 million in the past 48 hours, per DeFiLlama.\
- Liquidations were attributed to poor timing amid ecosystem exploits, socially engineered attacks, and rapid selling, making defense of positions impossible.\
- Kujira, a DeFi and fintech-focused Layer 1 blockchain on the Cosmos SDK, launched in July 2022 and hit an all-time high TVL on March 8, 2024.\
- The foundation proposes forming a DAO to manage the treasury and core protocol, migrating the community pool to this DAO.\
- The migration will utilize Fuzion’s OTC Bonds product, offering the community vested KUJI at a discounted rate.

news.headline: JPMorgan Sees Concerns for Ethereum Blockchain After the Merge
Ethereum Classic, Ravencoin, and Ergo Hash Rate Soar Post-Merge
Audius Launches Solana Token Airdrop for Artists as Music Streams Top 250 Million
news.summary:
- Audius, a crypto-powered music streaming service, is distributing 30 million AUDIO tokens (worth over $3 million) as part of a new airdrop campaign celebrating 250 million on-chain song streams.\
- The "Artist Appreciation Airdrop" kicks off the campaign, with 2 million AUDIO tokens allocated to 1,174 artists based on streams, sales, revenue, and followers.\
- The airdrop season will include weekly rewards for both artists and listeners, marking the first such campaign since 2020.\
- Audius CEO Roneil Rumburg emphasized the platform's commitment to artists and its role in the evolving music industry, highlighting the readiness for crypto-based payments and rewards.\
- Audius operates on Ethereum for governance but uses Solana for monetization, enabling it to scale to 250 million streams; the airdrop will occur on Solana.\
- The campaign will distribute 3% of Audius's 1.5 billion token supply, with potential plans for a second season of rewards.

news.headline: Web3 Watch: Hippo-themed ‘Moo Deng’ memecoin soars to $255M market cap
What is Grevm 1.0? The ‘Ultra-Fast’ Engine Powering Galxe’s Gravity Blockchain
Galxe launches alpha mainnet of its Layer 1 Gravity chain
news.summary:
- Galxe has launched the alpha mainnet of its Layer 1 blockchain, Gravity, integrating all its products for a cohesive user experience for over 25 million active users.\
- Gravity aims to solve Web3 fragmentation with seamless cross-chain interactions and full-chain abstraction, enhancing user experience.\
- Built as a proof-of-stake blockchain using the Arbitrum Nitro stack, Gravity is EVM compatible and employs Reth as its execution layer and Jolteon (AptosBFT) for consensus, promising high throughput and near-instant finality.\
- The blockchain supports G token native staking and restaking protocols, uniting a cross-spectrum community to safeguard the Gravity chain.\
- G token will serve as the native gas token, governance driver, growth incentive, and payment means within the Galxe ecosystem, with chain-agnostic gas payments supported.\
- The fully operational Gravity Mainnet is set to launch in Q2 2025; Galxe raised $10 million in 2022 from Multicoin Capital and Dragonfly Capital.

news.headline: Someone built an MEV bot to frontrun the Wayfinder PROMPT airdrop claims on Kaito, stealing $200,000 in ETH from ‘yappers’
'Off the Grid' Game Adds Bored Ape Avatars After GUN Token Launch
news.summary:
- Gunzilla Games introduced a Bored Ape Yacht Club-themed content pack for its battle royale game "Off the Grid" as part of an “Ape-ril” promotion, featuring new skins, guns, and emotes for Pro subscribers.\
- The content pack includes two Bored Ape-inspired avatars and two Ape-themed guns, with emotes allowing players to celebrate in-game achievements.\
- A limited edition “Ape-fool Mask” was available for players who participated on April Fool’s Day, adding a rare item to the game.\
- The launch follows the recent rollout of Gunzilla’s GUNZ mainnet and GUN token, which has seen a significant price drop since its peak, currently valued at $0.055.\
- Despite the token's decline, its fully diluted value is higher than other notable gaming tokens like ApeCoin and Parallel’s PRIME.\
- Off the Grid has not yet implemented the GUN token and GUNZ mainnet, with the migration being a priority but no set timeline for completion.

news.headline: Magma Devs Launches RPC Smart Router on Google Cloud
news.summary:
- Magma Devs launched its RPC Smart Router on the Google Cloud Platform (GCP) Marketplace on Monday, December 22, in partnership with Google.\
- The tool distributes blockchain requests across multiple providers in real time to reduce outages and delays when connecting to networks like Ethereum, Solana, and Polygon.\
- The Smart Router has already been tested during traffic spikes and provider outages, including use by custody provider Fireblocks.\
- Magma Devs Chairman Yair Cleper emphasized that the router offers a path to "production-grade blockchain infrastructure" for enterprises facing business-critical RPC failures.\
- The open-source software allows customers to define routing policies and connect to Lava Network’s decentralized RPC infrastructure or plug in other existing RPC providers.\
- Cleper clarified that Magma Devs deploys the software but does not host the service; customer responsibility remains for maintaining redundancy across different environments if a single cloud provider fails.

news.headline: Blockchain Infra Provider Lava Network Unveils “Industry First” Incentive Structure
Every Game on Ethereum Scaler Immutable in the Epic Games Store
Nomura's Laser Digital acquires digital asset trading firm Elysium Technology
Akash Network to deprecate its Cosmos chain, begin search for new network
news.summary:
- Akash will sunset its Cosmos SDK chain and migrate to a new, more secure network, with no specific timeline announced.  \
- The project is conducting a transparent, community-driven evaluation of potential new chains, including Solana as a strong contender.  \
- The migration aims to preserve core integrations like Osmosis and Keplr, while evolving staking into a potentially superior system.  \
- The move reflects broader trends in DePIN and AI compute, emphasizing security, liquidity, and community for scalable onchain demand.  \
- Akash’s transition is a major architectural shift, with the process expected to be open and debated before final decisions.  \
- The project is exploring networks that can support interoperability, throughput, and real-world AI workloads amid industry-wide challenges.

news.headline: Namada blockchain completes mainnet rollout for composable privacy layer as native token set to begin trading
news.summary:
- Namada, a Cosmos-based privacy-focused Layer 1 blockchain, has completed its mainnet rollout, introducing a composable privacy layer for decentralized apps.  \
- The launch coincides with a free-market token distribution, with NAM token trading expected to start this week on various exchanges.  \
- The network features a multi-asset shielded pool enabling private transactions and asset concealment within decentralized exchange liquidity pools.  \
- Namada plans to expand cross-chain support, including Bitcoin via Lombard and Babylon, Ethereum, and Solana through bridges, plus shielded swaps on Osmosis.  \
- Users will be able to perform shielded DeFi, gaming, and payments activities, with cross-chain swaps offering increased privacy and simplicity.  \
- The project has raised $60 million from investors like Polychain Capital and CMCC, with ongoing integrations to enhance cross-chain privacy capabilities.

news.headline: Yield Suffers $3.7M Loss Due to ‘Unintended’ Stablecoin Swap
news.summary:
- Yield Protocol (YO) experienced a loss of approximately $3.73 million on January 13, 2026, following a vault swap.\
- The swap converted about $3.84 million worth of stkGHO (staked Aave GHO) into only ~$122,000 in USDC.\
- Security firms suggest the trade involved extreme slippage and may have bypassed standard protections, possibly routing through a Uniswap v4 pool.\
- The incident is characterized as an "unintended swap" and not a smart contract exploit or hack.\
- YO proposed the recipient keep 10% of the proceeds as a bug bounty and return the rest.\
- Following the event, the YO team bought back $3.7 million worth of GHO and temporarily paused the YoUSD market on Pendle.

news.headline: EOS founder Dan Larimer predicts biblical apocalypse next week
news.summary:
- Daniel Larimer, founder of EOS, BitShares, and Steemit, predicts a biblical apocalypse on October 9, 2024, citing the ‘Revelations’ prophecy.\
- Larimer warns followers to prepare for “sudden destruction” and has released a YouTube video explaining his prediction.\
- He published a Biblical Tribulation Survival Guide, detailing nuclear war and a potential asteroid impact in 2029.\
- Larimer suggests that false alarms are preferable to ignoring significant celestial events.\
- Historically, such apocalyptic predictions often result in revised dates when they fail to materialize.

news.headline: Polkadot Registers Trademark for Blockchain Communication Platform
IDEX unveils zkEVM Layer 2 chain based on Polygon Supernets
US gov moves $33 million in seized crypto from FTX to unknown addresses: Arkham
news.summary:
- The U.S. government transferred $33.6 million in crypto from its "FTX Alameda Seized Funds" wallets to two unidentified addresses, according to Arkham data.\
- The transferred amount includes 5,024 ether (approx. $18 million) and 13.5 million BUSD.\
- Additional tokens moved include $1.5 million in SHIB and various other tokens such as AERGO, POWR, AXS, YFI, WBTC, NMR, COMP, SRM, and RLC.\
- The transfers occurred amid a market-wide crypto rally, with Ether trading at $3,643, up from $2,460 a month ago.\
- Ethereum's onchain volume in November reached $183.74 billion, the highest monthly volume recorded year-to-date.\
- The Block Research attributes the volume increase to capital rotation, as market participants shift funds from centralized exchanges (CEXs) to onchain activities.

news.headline: Argo's H1 Revenue Soars but Profits Drop as Bitcoin's Halving Boosts Mining Costs
New Solana tool fixes the KYC bottleneck
news.summary:
- Solana introduces the Attestation Service (SAS), an open protocol enabling verifiable, reusable off-chain credentials stored on-chain, simplifying KYC and identity checks.  \
- SAS allows issuers like KYC providers, DAOs, and governments to sign attestations (e.g., age, residency) linked to wallet addresses, which dapps can verify without storing personal data.  \
- The system is privacy-preserving, frictionless, and permissionless, promoting a more seamless and compliant user experience across decentralized apps.  \
- Attestations can be used for various onchain functions, including lending, reputation, and governance, enhancing Solana’s ecosystem capabilities.  \
- Companies like Civic, Solid, and RNS.ID plan to integrate SAS for issuing compliance credentials and verified stamps next month.  \
- SAS aims to scale real-world identity verification on Solana without compromising privacy, UX, or decentralization as the network grows.

news.headline: Lightspeed Newsletter: Worldcoin’s reputation predicament
news.summary:
- Worldcoin's World ID initiative aims to verify unique human identities and prevent Sybil attacks.\
- The project faces challenges in building trust and reputation among the communities it serves due to its use of biometric data.\
- Worldcoin's method requires users to scan their irises at scanning kiosks to create a unique identifier.\
- The technology has the potential to prevent social media scams, aid airdrops, distribute UBI, and improve onchain governance.\
- Worldcoin emphasizes that World ID is a privacy product and does not link to personal information or data.\
- Alternative proof-of-humanity solutions like Bright ID, Privado ID, Idena, and Civic offer different approaches to identity verification.

news.headline: Crypto Long \u0026 Short: Risk Ratings: DeFi’s Maturity Test
Can Stablecoins Break Free From the US Dollar?
UQUID integrates USDT on TRON for seamless public transport payments in Argentina
NFTs as Building Blocks for Ethereum’s Future
news.summary:
- Ethereum community is excited about ERC-8004, a new standard for "Trustless Agents" that adds an onchain trust layer for AI agent collaboration.  \
- The proposal suggests using NFTs for agent minting, ownership, and transfer, highlighting NFTs as infrastructure tools beyond collectibles.  \
- NFTs are increasingly used in AI for managing, discovering, and trading agents, with projects like Onchain Gaias and Virtuals Protocol leading the trend.  \
- Broader NFT infrastructure examples include Ethereum domain names (ENS), Uniswap LP positions, and Liquity debt positions, showcasing NFTs as portable, composable assets.  \
- Vitalik Buterin expressed surprise at NFTs' rise, emphasizing their potential to quietly reshape onchain interactions and infrastructure.  \
- The future of Ethereum may hinge more on "infra tokens" that enable app-layer functionalities than on tradable tokens alone.

news.headline: Bitcoin-backed stablecoins just got real — but tax rules still point some to custodians
Ondo Global Markets Taps Chainlink for US Stock Price Feeds
news.summary:
- Ondo Global Markets has partnered with Chainlink to use Data Feeds for onchain pricing of tokenized U.S. stocks and ETFs.\
- The integration aims to secure the pricing for assets like SPYon, QQQon, and TSLAon using Chainlink's decentralized oracle networks.\
- The live Chainlink feeds are already securing DeFi applications, such as Euler, for borrowing against tokenized stocks used as collateral.\
- The partnership enables tokenized securities to function reliably as productive collateral in lending markets, vaults, and structured products.\
- This move reinforces the integration of traditional securities data into decentralized finance (DeFi) infrastructure.

news.headline: mXRP, a yield-bearing tokenized XRP product, expands to BNB Chain via Lista DAO
news.summary:
- mXRP, a yield-bearing tokenized XRP product by Midas, is expanding to BNB Chain via Lista DAO, enabling XRP holders to access DeFi yields.  \
- The integration allows mXRP to be used as collateral on Lista DAO, combining base strategy returns with additional DeFi yields.  \
- Launched in September, mXRP is an ERC-20 token representing XRP plus returns from market-neutral trading and lending strategies.  \
- Midas is issuing mXRP natively on BNB Chain to improve user experience, with cross-chain functionality expected soon via Axelar.  \
- About 480,000 XRP holders on BNB Chain hold roughly $800 million in wrapped XRP, now gaining new yield opportunities.  \
- Midas’ total value locked across products exceeds $1.2 billion, with current mXRP TVL around $25 million.

news.headline: Synthetic stablecoin USDX depegs below $0.60, PancakeSwap and Lista monitoring the situation
news.summary:
- Stable Labs’ USDX stablecoin depegged below $0.60 on November 1, raising concerns among protocols like Lista DAO and PancakeSwap, which are monitoring the situation.  \
- USDX, a synthetic stablecoin with a $683 million circulating supply, initially spiked above $1.11 but has since fallen sharply, with current trading around $0.62.  \
- The depeg may be linked to the recent $128 million Balancer exploit and forced liquidations of Stable Labs’ hedged BTC/ETH shorts, causing redemption spikes.  \
- Allegations suggest that USDX collateral was drained to swap into other stablecoins, with some borrowing at 100% interest rates and no repayment plans.  \
- Lista DAO has moved to liquidate its USDX/USD1 vault to limit losses, executing a flash loan to recover over 2.9 million USD1 tokens.  \
- Stable Labs has not yet commented publicly; the situation remains fluid and under close watch by involved protocols.

news.headline: A deeper look at the HT to HTX token switcheroo
news.summary:
- HTX exchange has taken away the benefits associated with its HT token and transferred them to a new token supposedly owned by a DAO.\
- The DAO does not seem to exist yet, and it is unclear if anyone outside of HTX has decision-making power.\
- The move appears to benefit HTX at the expense of HT token holders.\
- The HT token price has fallen by half since the announcement.\
- The token conversion process is convoluted and opaque, with conversion rates varying over time and by the amount.\
- HT token holders are only receiving a small percentage of the new token supply, resulting in a dilution of 400% if all token holders convert.\
- The token maneuver may be an attempt to appease regulators and recapture value for HTX, while token holders are required to perform tasks on HTX to retain some value.\
- The situation raises questions about the decentralization claims and the transparency of the HTX DAO.

news.headline: Huobi Token Jumps 25% as HT Trading Volume Surges Overnight
news.summary:
- Huobi Token (HT) has surged 25% to $2.95, reaching a five-month high, due to increased trading volume.\
- HT broke a 12-month downtrend, signaling a potential bull market in the wider crypto market.\
- Daily trading volume for HT reached $32 million, the highest since February.\
- The rise in price has no clear news catalyst, but the altcoin market has been optimistic about a potential BTC ETF approval by the SEC.\
- The cryptocurrency market cap, excluding BTC, has risen from $505 billion to $631 billion since October 20.\
- Bitcoin is currently trading at a 16-month high of $36,800.

news.headline: A Top-5 US Hospital Is Exploring Blockchain for Patient Data
Coinbase integrates DEX trading within its exchange for US users on Base network
news.summary:
- Coinbase officially launched DEX trading for U.S. users (excluding New York) on October 9, after teasing the feature in August.  \
- The new service allows users to trade tokens before they are listed on the main exchange, directly within the Coinbase app.  \
- Coinbase integrated with liquidity pools supported by 1inch and 0x for secure, non-custodial onchain token swaps.  \
- At launch, users can trade Base-native tokens from projects like Virtuals AI Agents, Reserve Protocol DTFs, and Auki Labs.  \
- The DEX rollout follows a Q2 trading volume of $237 billion, up from $226 billion in the same period last year.  \
- The feature enhances Coinbase’s DeFi capabilities amid ongoing efforts to boost trading activity.

news.headline: Coinbase launches DEX trading for US users amid volume decline
news.summary:
- Coinbase announced the launch of DEX trading for US users, initially on Ethereum Layer 2 Base, excluding New York, enabling trading of millions of assets instantly after creation.  \
- The move follows a Q2 decline in Coinbase’s spot trading volumes and revenue, as the company aims to expand into tokenized stocks, prediction markets, and early-stage token sales.  \
- Users will be able to trade on DEXes like Uniswap and Aerodrome directly within the Coinbase app, with plans to add more networks such as Solana over time.  \
- Coinbase shares have fallen from over $400 in July to around $304 as of August 8, 2025, amid increased competition from Kraken and Robinhood.  \
- The new platform will support trading of Base-native tokens for projects like Virtuals AI Agents, Reserve Protocol, and Auki Labs.  \
- Coinbase is positioning itself as an “everything exchange” to counteract volume declines and market share erosion.

news.headline: Is crypto still early in the cycle?
Trump's FDIC Chief Rethinks Crypto Guidance as U.S. Senators Probe Debanking
Société Générale’s SG-FORGE Issues First Tokenized Bond in U.S.
news.summary:
- Société Générale’s SG-FORGE has issued the first tokenized bond in the U.S., marking a significant step in digital asset adoption in traditional finance.  \
- The bond issuance aims to enhance liquidity and accessibility for institutional investors through blockchain technology.  \
- This move follows increasing interest from banks and regulators in integrating tokenized assets into mainstream financial markets.  \
- The tokenized bond is part of SG-FORGE’s broader strategy to innovate within the fixed income space using blockchain.  \
- The development signals growing acceptance of crypto-based financial instruments in regulated environments as of November 2025.  \
- The article also highlights ongoing crypto market activity, with Bitcoin near $93,750 and Ethereum around $3,157.

news.headline: Crypto Daybook Americas: A Bizarre $5.4B Loss Has Left Investors Devastated
What is SmarDex? The DeFi Disruptor Poised to Take Over the DEX Market
Former Tether CEO to debut rival, yield-bearing stablecoin on Ethereum and Solana: Bloomberg
news.summary:
- Reeve Collins, a co-founder and former CEO of Tether, plans to launch a new yield-bearing stablecoin called Pi Protocol on Ethereum and Solana by the second half of 2025.\
- Unlike traditional stablecoins like USDT and USDC, Pi Protocol will offer yields potentially earned from tokenized real-world assets, similar to newer stablecoins like Ethena and M^0.\
- The exact mechanism for generating yield is not detailed, but it may involve revenues from tokenized assets such as U.S. Treasuries, akin to Mountain Protocol's USDM which offers around 5% annual yield.\
- Collins, who played a key role in Tether's early development and its association with Bitfinex, has a history of innovation in the crypto space, including the creation of one of the first NFT platforms, BLOCKv.\
- Tether remains the most actively traded cryptocurrency with a market cap of $141 billion, and reported over $13 billion in net profits in 2024 from its reserve assets.

news.headline: Crypto Markets Drop on U.S. Labor Market Concerns
Ethereum Bounces as Grayscale’s Ether ETF Posts First Daily Inflow
news.summary:
- Ethereum (ETH) rallied 1.7% to $2,635 as Grayscale Ethereum Trust (ETHE) recorded its first day without outflows since launching.\
- ETHE saw $4.93 million in net inflows on Monday, marking the fifth positive inflow day for Ether ETFs since July 23.\
- Bitcoin (BTC) posted a modest 0.11% gain to $58,882, while Polkadot (DOT) fell by 2%, and Solana (SOL) rose 0.12%.\
- Sats (1000SATS) led the top 100 cryptocurrencies with a 15% gain, while Lido DAO (LDO) was the worst performer, dropping 5%.\
- Analysts remain cautious despite the partial rebound, noting that Bitcoin and Ethereum need to reclaim key resistance levels above $60,000 and $2,700 respectively.\
- U.S. stock futures rose as inflation data showed a slower-than-expected increase, with the producer price index at 0.1%. Investors await consumer price index (CPI) data for further market guidance.

news.headline: Stablecoin-focused Noble protocol will sunset Cosmos appchain and launch EVM-based Layer 1
news.summary:
- Stablecoin-focused appchain Noble is sunsetting its Cosmos SDK-based blockchain to launch a standalone EVM Layer 1, named Noble EVM, scheduled for March 18, 2026.\
- Founder Jelena Djuric cited the move to EVM as necessary to build a "performant stack" and a strong base product layer for stablecoin and FX infrastructure builders.\
- Noble has historically served as an intermediary for real-world asset issuers like Circle and Ondo Finance on Cosmos, processing over $22 billion in transaction volume since 2023.\
- The new Noble EVM will feature sub-second finality and leverage the Commonware stack, focusing on real end-user stablecoin applications and active DeFi partnerships.\
- Noble’s Treasury-backed, yield-paying USDN stablecoin will be a central feature, integrated into a managed vault utilizing a Pendle strategy on HyperEVM.\
- The new chain will also anchor a novel DeFi protocol for FX swaps between USD and EUR stablecoins, including capital-efficient FX perpetuals.

news.headline: Stablecoin Platform M0 Raises $40 Million in Polychain-Led Series B
news.summary:
- Switzerland-based stablecoin infrastructure platform M0 raised $40 million in Series B funding, led by Polychain Capital and Ribbit Capital, bringing total funding to ~$100 million since 2023.  \
- The round also included backing from Endeavor Catalyst, Pantera Capital, and Bain Capital Crypto.  \
- M0’s platform separates reserve management from programmability, enabling regulated entities to hold assets like cash and Treasuries while developers control token movement.  \
- As of July, M0’s network supported over $300 million in stablecoin supply, more than doubling since January.  \
- The funding will support expansion of M0’s “layer-zero” stablecoin network and attract more issuers amid rising interest in dollar-pegged crypto following U.S. stablecoin regulations enacted earlier this year.  \
- M0’s technology underpins projects like MetaMask’s mUSD, Noble, Usual, and gaming firm Playtron.

news.headline: The Daily: Crypto market braces for Trump’s ‘Liberation Day’ impact, FDUSD stablecoin depegs after Justin Sun raises solvency concerns and more
news.summary:
- Crypto markets are on edge as President Trump is set to announce large-scale tariffs, potentially impacting Bitcoin and equity markets, with analysts predicting continued volatility and consolidation in the $75,000 to $88,000 range.\
- Tron founder Justin Sun has raised concerns about the solvency of First Digital Trust, leading to a temporary depegging of its FDUSD stablecoin by 9%, though the company claims it remains solvent and plans legal action to defend its reputation.\
- Elon Musk's X, along with other groups, has petitioned the U.S. Supreme Court to prevent the IRS from accessing Coinbase user data without a warrant, citing privacy violations.\
- Ripple's RLUSD stablecoin has launched on Kraken, with a market cap nearing $250 million, and is being integrated into Ripple's payment platform for cross-border transactions.\
- Usual, a decentralized stablecoin protocol, has partnered with Sherlock to offer a $16 million bug bounty, the largest in crypto history, for critical vulnerabilities.

news.headline: Swarm Intelligence \u0026 Crypto AI: Inside Fortytwo’s Decentralized Inference Network | Fully Diluted
Morning Minute: DraftKings Bets Big on Prediction Markets
news.summary:
- DraftKings is acquiring Railbird, a CFTC-regulated prediction market platform, to launch DraftKings Predictions, initially covering finance, culture, and elections, with sports markets likely to follow.\
- The move signals mainstream acceptance of prediction markets, leveraging DraftKings’ 6M users, regulatory legitimacy, and trusted brand to compete with crypto-native platforms like Polymarket.\
- Crypto markets declined slightly today, with Bitcoin down 1% at $107,800; Bitcoin and ETH briefly rose amid gold’s dip, and Galaxy reported an $505M profit amid strong inflows.\
- Bitcoin ETFs saw $477.2M in inflows on Tuesday, while Hong Kong approved the first spot Solana ETF, and BlackRock reported $3B in Bitcoin ETF trades since July.\
- Meme coins mostly declined, with BSC memes dominating volume; notable movers included EVAA (+80%) and XPIN (-37%).\
- NFT prices mostly fell, with notable drops in Punks, Pudgy, and BAYC; NBA Top Shot announced new scarcity-focused updates ahead of the season.

news.headline: GameFi Tokens Axie Infinity, The Sandbox Defy Crypto's Bearish Trend
news.summary:
- Axie Infinity surged 88.5% this week, significantly outpacing Bitcoin's lower trading, following a major tokenomics overhaul.\
- The Sandbox recorded a 15.3% rally over the past week, while Decentraland and Smooth Love Potion gained 4.4% and 0.7%, respectively.\
- One analysis suggests the gaming token rally is due to localized project catalysts, specifically Axie Infinity's restructuring efforts.\
- An opposing view attributes the short-term defiance to capital rotation and high-risk speculation away from meme coins.\
- Experts agree a structural GameFi revival hinges on a fundamental increase in token utility and user demand within the games.\
- Despite the recent gains, the GameFi sector is still under pressure, showing a roughly 75% decline in 2025.

news.headline: Sky Mavis alleges Seed Go infringes on Axie Infinity IP
news.summary:
- Sky Mavis cofounder Alexander Larsen accused Sui-powered game Seed Go of IP infringement related to Axie Infinity.  \
- Sky Mavis has notified the Sui team and seeks to resolve the issue outside of court.  \
- Seed Go features NFT characters called “Seedmon,” a token called SLOVE, and is a Telegram mini-app inspired by Pokémon Go and Axie Infinity.  \
- Axie Infinity, launched in 2018, has generated over $1.3 billion in total revenue, with its core tokens SLP and AXS.  \
- Sui Foundation clarified it made a small investment in Seed but is not involved in its game development and does not endorse Seed.  \
- SeedDAO, supported by Mysten Labs, did not respond to requests for comment.

news.headline: Singapore’s biggest bank DBS introduces ‘Token Services’ to enable blockchain-based banking
news.summary:
- DBS Bank, Singapore's largest bank by assets, has launched "DBS Token Services" to enhance blockchain-based banking for institutional clients.\
- The new services integrate tokenization and smart contract capabilities with DBS's existing banking infrastructure, including an Ethereum Virtual Machine-compatible permissioned blockchain.\
- Key features include Treasury Tokens for multi-currency transactions, Conditional Payments to improve payment workflows, and Programmable Rewards for digital voucher programs.\
- The initiative aims to optimize liquidity management, streamline operations, and unlock new engagement opportunities for businesses and public sector entities.\
- DBS's use of a permissioned blockchain ensures compliance while leveraging blockchain benefits.\
- The bank's Treasury Tokens, introduced in August with Ant International, allow 24/7 multi-currency intra-group settlements.

news.headline: ‘Doom Olympics’ Game Competition Kicks Off on Ethereum
news.summary:
- The "Doom Olympics," a fully on-chain, weeklong competition, has kicked off on Ethereum with a $15,000 prize pool.\
- Organized by crypto gaming project RIVES, the event uses the Cartesi blockchain and Base, an Ethereum layer-2 network, to record and verify every in-game action.\
- The competition features seven unique challenges, including Knuckle Crusher and Treasure Seeker, and offers social activities like friend referrals.\
- Id Software, the original developer of Doom, is not involved; instead, the competition uses the open-source "Freedoom" version.\
- The event aims to ensure fairness and trust in speedrunning by recording every gameplay element on-chain, making it replayable and verifiable.\
- The competition runs until September 19, with the goal of eliminating cheating and enabling decentralized verification of speedruns and custom rule creation.

news.headline: CRYPTO ALL GREEN, PPI TODAY, PUMP \u0026 IP LEAD ALTS
news.summary:
- Crypto markets are mixed ahead of today’s PPI data; SOL outperforms while ETH ETFs see inflows after a 6-day outflow streak.  \
- Metaplanet upsizes its $1.4B funding round; KindlyMD commits $30M to the raise; Sharplink repurchases 1M shares.  \
- Ethena joins the race for the HYPE stablecoin, with Native Markets favored to win; CBOE plans perpetual futures for BTC and ETH.  \
- US regulators: Democrats seek SEC and CFTC support for a crypto bill; CFTC explores recognizing foreign crypto platforms.  \
- Major exchanges: Gemini plans an IPO this week; Robinhood to introduce copy trading; Grayscale files for HBAR, LTC, BCH investment vehicles.  \
- Binance and SGB to enable direct USD transfers for retail users; Vietnam approves a 5-year crypto trading pilot program.

news.headline: Flare is Building a ‘Fully Decentralized’ DeFi Hub: Co-Founder
Solana AI Tokens Look to Mount Comeback
news.summary:
- Small-cap Solana AI agent tokens like SwarmNodeai (SNAI), Heurist (HEU), and GRIFFAIN are rallying, with SNAI up nearly 230% over the past week.  \
- SNAI has fallen 90% from its $130 million market cap high; HEU is down 77% from its $460 million peak, and GRIFFAIN is down 93% from $625 million.  \
- Larger AI tokens such as AI16Z, GOAT, and VIRTUAL are declining today, with AI16Z down 10%, VIRTUAL down 5%, and GOAT down 4%.  \
- The rebound follows a broader altcoin rally over the weekend, amid renewed interest in AI-related projects on Solana.  \
- The recent price movements are fueling optimism around the pumpfun ecosystem, with PUMP, a memecoin launched via the same platform, up over 16% today and nearly 23% this week.  \
- The AI agent sector remains significantly below its all-time highs, despite recent gains in smaller tokens.

news.headline: DOGE, PEPE, PUMP Bleed As Pump.fun Confirms No Airdrop 'In Immediate Future'
news.summary:
- The meme coin market has declined over 10% in the past 24 hours, with Dogecoin, Shiba Inu, and Pepe each dropping around 7%.  \
- Pump.fun’s co-founder Alon Cohen confirmed that the highly anticipated PUMP airdrop will not happen in the “immediate future,” causing PUMP to fall 17.7% and exit the top 100 coins.  \
- The decline follows a cryptic social media post and a sharp drop in prediction odds for the airdrop, now just 3% by the end of July.  \
- Pump.fun’s token launched with $600 million raised in 12 minutes but has since lost 55% of its value, amid competition from Solana’s BONK platform.  \
- Major tokens created by Pump.fun, like GOAT, Moo Deng, and Peanut the Squirrel, are down double digits; only 12 of the top 100 meme coins are in the green.  \
- REKT, a brand coin linked to a drink company, is up 21.7% after partnering with Binance.US, with increased market interest from traders.

news.headline: Wrapped Bitcoin team taps Hyperlane for WBTC bridge between Ethereum and Solana
news.summary:
- Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) is expanding to the Solana ecosystem via the open-source interoperability network Hyperlane.\
- The integration utilizes Hyperlane's Nexus Bridge to allow holders to transfer WBTC between Ethereum and Solana.\
- WBTC currently holds an $8 billion market cap and maintains 1:1 reserves backed by Bitcoin held in custody by BitGo and BiT Global.\
- This expansion aims to provide developers and users with access to Bitcoin-backed liquidity on high-throughput networks.\
- In 2024, WBTC faced scrutiny after custodian BitGo adopted a "multi-jurisdictional, multi-institutional" custody model involving BiT Global, leading some entities like MakerDAO to distance themselves.\
- Despite the controversy, WBTC remains the largest tokenized version of bitcoin.

news.headline: Starknet Unveils 100M STRK Initiative to Bring Bitcoin DeFi to Its Network
news.summary:
- Starknet announced a $100 million STRK incentive program called “BTCFi Season” to boost Bitcoin DeFi activity on its network, with around $13 million in tokens allocated.  \
- The Layer 2 network launched trustless BTC staking, allowing users to stake BTC and earn rewards without losing custody, aiming to become the execution layer for Bitcoin using ZK tech.  \
- Re7 Capital is backing the initiative with a new BTC-denominated yield fund, targeting institutional Bitcoin use within Starknet’s ecosystem.  \
- Several contributors and cross-chain firms, including WBTC, Lombard, LayerZero, and Hyperlane, will support Bitcoin DeFi and cross-chain markets on Starknet.  \
- Starknet’s current TVL is over $625 million, making it the seventh-largest Ethereum rollup, with the overall Bitcoin DeFi TVL reaching $8.45 billion, dominated by protocols like Babylon.  \
- Following the announcement, STRK’s price surged nearly 9%, reflecting increased investor interest in Bitcoin DeFi on Starknet.

news.headline: The Daily: Stripe acquires stablecoin platform Bridge for $1.1 billion, crypto markets ‘scream risk-on’ and more
MetaMask and digital identity firm Space ID aim to simplify crypto
news.summary:
- MetaMask and Space ID have integrated to simplify crypto transactions and wallet access.\
- Users can now register a Space ID domain name to map all their crypto public addresses.\
- The integration allows replacing complex wallet addresses with a single, customizable handle for transactions on MetaMask.\
- Space ID, a web3 domain and identity platform, enables users to manage digital assets through one interface.\
- MetaMask Product Lead Christian Montoya highlighted the integration's potential to enhance ease and security for users on any EVM-compatible chain.\
- Space ID users can now organize and store handles for multiple chains in one wallet.

news.headline: Stablecoin protocol Gyroscope rolls out token and airdrop amid revenue growth
Gyroscope Rolls Out Yield-Bearing Version of Stablecoin Targeting Over 10% Yield
news.summary:
- Gyroscope has launched "Savings GYD" (sGYD), a yield-generating stablecoin targeting 12%-15% annualized yield, dependent on market conditions.\
- Revenue for sGYD comes from tokens backing assets in segregated vaults across various DeFi investment strategies and fees from high-yield liquidity pools.\
- The protocol aims to attract DAOs to allocate part of their treasuries in sGYD for yield generation.\
- The launch coincides with the start of "season 2" of Gyroscope's points earning program SPIN, offering users options to earn native yields or boost rewards.\
- Gyroscope's stablecoin is marketed as an "all-weather" token, backed by multiple stablecoins and automated market-making strategies to protect against stablecoin failures.\
- The project has raised $4.5 million in venture funding and currently has $29 million in total value locked on its platform, according to DefiLlama.

news.headline: AO and Arweave's Unique Approach to Modularity with Seth Bloomberg | The Story
Arweave releases testnet for ‘absurdly scalable’ compute layer designed for social media, AI
news.summary: Arweave has released a testnet for its highly scalable compute layer called Arweave AO. The team initially had the idea to use its data storage platform as the foundation for a scalable blockchain network in early 2020. However, it wasn't until they were working on plans for a social media network that they realized the need for a highly scalable network to support a marketplace for content ownership tokens. The team created an internal testnet for Arweave AO, which is now running on 220 machines. The main Arweave chain has seen significant activity, with 3 billion transactions and 1 billion in less than two months. Arweave AO splits the components of running a blockchain into separate scalable subnetworks and can support storing large amounts of data, such as AI models. The team plans to have multiple audits before launching the mainnet in 2024. Arweave AO is positioned as a potential competitor to Ethereum.

news.headline: Boundless unlocks Bitcoin settlement and verification for Ethereum and Base ZK proofs
news.summary:
- Boundless launched a cross-chain verification system enabling Bitcoin to act as a settlement and verification layer for computationally expensive zero-knowledge (ZK) proofs.\
- The system initially connects Ethereum mainnet and Base Layer 2 execution environments to Bitcoin's settlement layer.\
- Boundless CEO Shiv Shankar stated the goal is not to turn Bitcoin into Ethereum, but to secure "Ethereum-style execution" using Bitcoin as the ultimate verifier without changing Bitcoin's core protocol.\
- The verification framework utilizes the Bitcoin Virtual Machine (BitVM) and taps into the Bitcoin-native ZK-powered rollup, Citrea.\
- The development supports the growing need for strong, neutral settlement layers for Layer 2s amid increasing L1 competition.\
- Boundless plans to expand support for additional chains and ecosystem integrations beyond the initial EVM connections.

news.headline: Hivemapper raises $32M, launches new subscription for Bee dashcams
news.summary:
- Hivemapper (now Bee Maps) raised $32M to expand its DePIN mapping network, with investors including Pantera Capital and Borderless Capital.  \
- The company launched a new “Bee Membership” subscription, offering hardware, LTE connectivity, and fleet software for $19/month in the US, replacing a $589 upfront device cost.  \
- Funding aims to accelerate coverage expansion and contributor rewards to meet enterprise demand in navigation, automotive, and city planning sectors.  \
- Notable clients include Lyft, Volkswagen, NBCUniversal, Mapbox, and HERE, with some deals valued in the six to seven figures.  \
- Bee Maps leverages an edge-based AI pipeline to convert contributor imagery into dynamic maps, producing data 5-6x faster than traditional providers like Google and Apple.  \
- The network has mapped 644 million km of roads, including 21 million km of unique roadway, highlighting its rapid data refresh capabilities.

news.headline: DePIN Fees Hit All-Time High
news.summary:
- DePIN sector activity surged in August 2025, pushing fees to all-time highs over the past month.  \
- Helium, boosted by its partnership with Solana Seeker, dominates the sector, generating $330K-$370K weekly, about 60% of total fees.  \
- Geonet, Akash Network, and Hivemapper collectively contributed $181K (32%) of weekly DePIN fees.  \
- Despite rising fees, DePIN tokens like Helium, Render, and Filecoin have declined 40-70% over the past year.  \
- DePIN's market value is projected to reach $3.5 trillion by 2028, according to the World Economic Forum.  \
- Major telecom and infrastructure firms, including AT&T, are partnering with DePIN projects like Helium to expand decentralized wireless networks.

news.headline: New IMF Report Warns of Stablecoin Risk, Sparking Criticism From Experts
news.summary:
- The IMF released a 56-page report warning against the risks of stablecoin adoption, drawing parallels to concerns about threats to governmental monetary control.\
- The report advocates for Central Bank Digital Currencies (CBDCs), stating central bank money should maintain its role as the most resilient form of money.\
- Critics, including Gate CBO Kevin Lee, argue the "substitution risk" narrative misses the potential for private stablecoins and CBDCs to coexist.\
- The IMF cited risks like "fire sales" that could force central banks to intervene, threatening financial stability, mirroring concerns from the ECB and BIS.\
- Human Finance co-founder Erbil Karaman countered that stablecoins benefit populations in unstable fiat economies and that the report ignores centralized financial system failures.\
- The IMF also expressed concern that stablecoins could be exploited for illicit purposes like money laundering due to ease of cross-border transfer and low transaction costs.

news.headline: Stablecoins Power $670 Billion in Onchain Loans: Visa
news.summary:
- Over $670 billion in loans have been issued via on-chain stablecoin lending platforms over the past five years, with August 2025 seeing $51.7 billion in monthly volume.  \
- Stablecoins like USDC ($76B) and USDT ($181B) dominate the market, representing over 98% of stablecoin lending activity.  \
- Most lending activity occurs on Ethereum and Polygon (85%), with protocols like Aave ($40B TVL) and Compound ($2.6B TVL) accounting for 89% of volume.  \
- Stablecoins are increasingly used beyond payments, playing a key role in transforming global lending and capital markets.  \
- Platforms such as Morpho ($7B TVL, $1.7B/month loans) and Huma Finance ($500M cross-border lending) are leading institutional stablecoin lending networks.  \
- U.S. regulatory clarity, including the GENIUS Act, is supporting growth and legitimacy of stablecoin lending markets.

news.headline: Shiba Inu Soars Amid Memecoin Market Rebound
news.summary:
- Shiba Inu, the second-largest memecoin by market cap, surged nearly 10% in the past 24 hours, with its open interest increasing by 30% after a mysterious user burned 1 billion SHIB tokens.\
- The entire memecoin sector has gained approximately 20% in the past week, driven by a general market rebound and positive sentiment around memecoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs).\
- Top gainers in the memecoin market include SPX, FARTCOIN, and GIGA.\
- The resurgence in memecoins is partly attributed to bullish signals around memecoin ETFs, with applications for Dogecoin, Pengu, and Bonk from major asset managers.\
- A Shiba Inu core team member hinted at the potential for a SHIB ETF, highlighting its widespread availability and community-driven nature.

news.headline: Coinbase to Suspend Trading of Floki, Turbo, Gigachad in New York on April 14, 2025 at 2 PM ET
news.summary:
- Coinbase will suspend trading of three meme coins—Floki (FLOKI), Turbo (TURBO), and Gigachad (GIGA)—in New York starting April 14, 2025, at 2 PM ET.\
- The decision follows a review of Coinbase's listed assets to ensure compliance with its listing standards.\
- Despite the announcement, the market response has been muted, with Floki gaining 3.6%, Giga rising 1.1%, and Turbo increasing by 5.4% in the last 24 hours.\
- The SEC has clarified that meme coins typically do not qualify as securities under U.S. law.\
- Legal experts suggest the suspension may be linked to a pending legal case in New York, reflecting Coinbase's efforts to adapt to regulatory and market changes.

news.headline: Crypto Market Today: OG, ASTR Surge as Bitcoin Defends $112K
news.summary:
- Bitcoin held support at $112,000 but remains below its 50-day moving average, indicating near-term weakness.  \
- The CoinDesk 20 Index rose over 1%, reflecting broader market resilience.  \
- OG (Zero Gravity) token surged over 50% after its mainnet launch, with Avalanche's AVAX up 10%, driven by hype around its AI-focused layer-1 blockchain.  \
- Global futures open interest declined from $110B to $102B, but open interest in AVAX, MNT, NEAR, and XMR increased, signaling bullish momentum.  \
- A short squeeze potential exists as perpetual futures show negative funding rates for XRP, BNB, ADA, LINK, and SHIB.  \
- Gold surged 44% in 2025, outperforming bitcoin, with silver, platinum, and palladium also posting significant gains amid major central bank gold purchases.

news.headline: Fan Token Firm Chiliz Acquires 2-Time 'Dota 2' Champions, OG Esports
news.summary:
- Chiliz, a fan token platform, has acquired OG Esports, two-time Dota 2 champions, marking its entry into the competitive gaming scene.  \
- The deal aims to leverage OG’s esports success to expand Chiliz’s influence in digital fan engagement and tokenization.  \
- Chiliz’s move signals growing interest in integrating blockchain with esports and gaming communities.  \
- The acquisition could boost Chiliz’s presence in the rapidly expanding esports industry, valued at over $1 billion.  \
- No financial details of the acquisition were disclosed.  \
- The deal underscores the increasing crossover between crypto platforms and mainstream entertainment sectors.

news.headline: How crypto airdrops will change in 2026
news.summary:
- Crypto projects are shifting airdrop distributions toward rewarding on-chain activity like trading volume and deposits to combat Sybil attacks.\
- Hyperliquid’s successful "play-for-points" airdrop model has been emulated by numerous competitors, including inspiring Polymarket's upcoming token launch.\
- The resurgence of Initial Coin Offerings (ICOs) under the current US administration may cause some projects to opt for token sales over airdrops, potentially reducing overall airdrop frequency.\
- ICOs generate direct capital, whereas airdrops offer indirect incentives, leading some projects to prioritize ICOs to avoid "leaving money on the table."\
- For projects conducting both, the allocation balance may shift toward ICOs, resulting in smaller airdrops for users, as seen with Monad’s recent distribution.\
- User trust in airdrops is declining due to incidents like the Apriori airdrop, which saw 80% of tokens claimed by a single clustered group in an apparent Sybil attack.

news.headline: Chainlink launches ’24/5′ onchain data streams for tokenized US stocks and ETFs
Orderly Network Introduces Build-Your-Own Perp DEX Platform
news.summary:
- Orderly Network has launched a customizable perpetual DEX platform, allowing users to build their own decentralized trading experience.  \
- The platform aims to enhance flexibility and user control in derivatives trading within the crypto ecosystem.  \
- This development follows increasing demand for tailored DeFi solutions and more modular trading infrastructure.  \
- No specific launch date or timeline for widespread adoption was provided in the article.  \
- The move highlights ongoing innovation in decentralized exchanges and derivatives platforms in 2025.  \
- Market reaction or user adoption metrics are not yet available.

news.headline: AI Sector Rebounds as Agent Payment Systems Gain Traction
news.summary:
- AI coins, led by Virtuals and x402 protocols, are experiencing a strong rebound, with Virtuals up 84% last week and key tokens like TIBBIR and AIXBT up 27% and 54%.  \
- Coinbase's recent support for Virtuals-based AI agent tokens on October 24 helped catalyze the rally.  \
- Virtuals' token surged over 10,000% from October 2024 to January 2025, reaching $5, before retracing to $1.46 today.  \
- x402 protocols, enabling AI agent payment systems, are seeing explosive growth, with total transactions rising from 60,000 in early October to 1.51 million now.  \
- PayAI’s token has surged 2000% over the past month, reflecting increased interest in AI-led finance.  \
- Industry experts warn that much of the recent speculative gains may not be sustainable, emphasizing that only projects close to commercialization are likely to survive long-term.

news.headline: AI crypto bot AIXBT lost $100,000 worth of ETH after hacker gained unauthorized ‘dashboard access’
news.summary:
- AIXBT, an AI crypto market commentary bot, lost approximately $100,000 worth of ETH due to a "dashboard access hack" that exploited its simulation wallet.\
- The attack involved malicious prompting, leading AIXBT to send around 55 ETH to the attacker, but did not affect the bot's core systems.\
- The bot, built on the Virtuals Protocol, is known for analyzing market sentiment and real-time analytics, accessible via AIXBT tokens.\
- The team behind AIXBT has paused the data dashboard, swapped servers, and migrated keys to enhance security and prevent further breaches.\
- AIXBT's token value dropped over 21% to about $0.094 following the incident, as reported by The Block.\
- This event highlights the risks associated with AI experimentation in crypto, with AIXBT being part of a trend alongside other bots like ai16z and Truth Terminal.

news.headline: Binance Wallet unlocks in-app leveraged crypto futures trading with Aster team-up
news.summary:
- Binance Wallet has integrated with decentralized exchange Aster to enable users to trade leveraged crypto futures directly within the wallet.\
- This integration allows users to maintain self-custody of their assets while trading futures, addressing concerns highlighted by past CeFi exchange collapses.\
- The feature is currently available on the Binance Wallet web interface for users operating on the BNB Smart Chain.\
- Users can utilize collateral such as BNB, USDT, and Bitcoin for leveraged contracts.\
- Aster also supports contracts tied to traditional equities like Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA), as well as the Invesco QQQ ETF, enabling broader diversification for wallet users.\
- Aster reported a 24-hour trading volume of $6.74 billion at the time of the announcement, significantly less than market leader Hyperliquid's $8.46 billion.

news.headline: Ondo Finance Rolls Out Tokenized U.S. Stocks, ETFs as Equity Tokenization Ramps Up
YZi Labs-backed perps DEX Vooi launches V2 mainnet, enabling cross-chain transactions via chain abstraction
news.summary:
- Vooi, backed by YZi Labs (Binance family office), has achieved $8.4 billion in cumulative perp trading volume since May 2024.  \
- The platform’s V2 mainnet uses OneBalance’s chain abstraction toolkit to eliminate the need for chains, bridges, and native gas fees.  \
- VooI V2 aims to simplify access by removing wallets, bridges, and multiple dashboards, targeting easier retail trading.  \
- It offers cross-chain perpetual trading across multiple exchanges like Hyperliquid, Orderly, and SynFutures via a unified interface.  \
- The platform supports EVM-compatible networks such as Ethereum, BNB Chain, Arbitrum, Optimism, and Base, with plans to expand to non-EVM ecosystems.  \
- VooI’s approach sets a new standard for DeFi access by unifying liquidity and removing traditional barriers to entry.

news.headline: Base DEX SynFutures Rolls Out AI Trading Agent
Arbitrum Token Sets Record High as Value Locked Crosses $2.5B
news.summary:
- Arbitrum's ARB token reached a record high of nearly $2, with total value locked (TVL) surpassing $2.5 billion.\
- The token's price has increased by 10% in the past 24 hours, outperforming the broader crypto market.\
- On-chain volumes on Arbitrum-based applications have exceeded $920 million.\
- The influx of users to Arbitrum has surpassed Solana-based applications, which experienced a surge in volumes due to a meme coin frenzy in December.\
- The TVL on Arbitrum-based applications has grown by $1 billion since October, accompanied by an increase in net inflows.\
- Token incentives provided by Arbitrum have contributed to the ecosystem's popularity, with decentralized exchanges GMX and Gains Network offering increased rewards and lower fees.

news.headline: Arbitrum DAO approves $23 million ‘backfund’ for projects that missed initial grant
news.summary: The Arbitrum DAO has approved a $23.54 million "backfund" to support 26 projects that missed out on its initial round of grant funding. The initiative aims to develop and incentivize the use of the projects' applications on the Ethereum Layer 2 network. The funds will be dispersed to the projects that were approved for a grant from the Ethereum Layer 2 network's Short-Term Incentive Program (STIP) but failed to receive the funds due to budget constraints. The largest grant will go to Gains Network, receiving 4.5 million tokens ($5 million). Other top grants include Stargate Finance and Synapse, each receiving 2 million tokens ($2.2 million), and Wormhole, receiving 1.8 million tokens ($2 million). Some projects that received funding in the initial round opposed the additional funding proposal, suggesting a second round of proposals instead.

news.headline: Hacker returns 70% of $21 million taken from Transit Swap DEX
Bitcoin gets removed from top slot on CoinMarketCap
FET Drops Over 10% as Fetchai Pushes Legal Action Against Ocean Protocol
news.summary:
- Fetchai alleges Ocean Protocol converted 661 million OCEAN tokens into 286 million FET in July, then moved most to exchanges, causing market impact and losses for FET holders.  \
- FET has dropped over 70% since July, losing about 13% since Fetchai’s Oct. 21 tweet, amid the controversy.  \
- Ocean Protocol exited the Artificial Superintelligence Alliance (ASI) in October, after converting 81% of OCEAN tokens into FET since July 2024, without prior warning.  \
- Fetchai plans legal action against Ocean Protocol, claiming deliberate actions to harm FET holders; Ocean dismisses claims as baseless and ongoing legal proceedings are in place.  \
- Blockchain analytics confirm Ocean converted large amounts of OCEAN into FET and sent tokens to Binance and OTC firms; unclear if tokens were sold or liquidated.  \
- Fetchai offers $250,000 for info on OceanDAO signatories, as the dispute intensifies over token transfers and project transparency.

news.headline: Ocean Protocol Withdraws From Artificial Superintelligence Alliance
news.summary:
- Ocean Protocol has exited the Artificial Superintelligence Alliance (ASI) effective immediately, citing a desire for independent funding and control over its tokenomics.  \
- The move allows OCEAN to de-peg from FET and trade independently again, with a bridge remaining open for conversions at 0.433226 FET per OCEAN.  \
- Ocean plans to use profits from its spin-out ventures for buybacks and burns of OCEAN, reducing supply permanently.  \
- About 270 million OCEAN (19% of total supply) remains unconverted, held by over 37,000 addresses and trading on major exchanges.  \
- The ASI Alliance and Fetch.ai described the split as amicable, emphasizing ongoing collaboration and shared vision for decentralized AI infrastructure.  \
- Ocean’s withdrawal does not impact the alliance’s technology, operations, or overall mission.

news.headline: ADA Takes a Hard Fall as Traders Feel the Heat of War in the Middle East
news.summary:
- ADA dropped 6.45% over 24 hours to $0.5478 amid escalating Middle East tensions, briefly hitting $0.5464 on a volume spike.\
- 24-hour trading volume was 37.37% above its 30-day average, with the steepest decline during the 21:00 hour, falling 3.2% on 126 million volume.\
- Despite volatility, nearly $1 billion worth of ADA has been withdrawn from centralized exchanges in 2024, and over 310 million tokens have been accumulated by large holders in June.\
- Institutional interest is evident with Ford advising on a Cardano-based legal data storage pilot involving Iagon and Cloud Court, aiming to enhance secure legal data management.\
- Technical analysis shows ADA declined from $0.586 to $0.545, forming a bearish descending channel, but a brief bullish channel emerged around $0.554 with volume support.\
- The project highlights Cardano’s expanding enterprise presence, with potential applications across healthcare, finance, and public sectors.

news.headline: NIM for DYM: Dymension Stakers Get First Confirmed Airdrop
news.summary:
- Dymension, a layer-1 blockchain network, launched a token airdrop in February worth $390 million.\
- DYM token stakers can now claim an airdrop from NIM Network, a RollApp powered by Dymension.\
- NIM Network's "Genesis Rolldrop" offers 90 million NIM tokens, with over 500,000 eligible wallets.\
- The airdrop is also open to holders of certain NFTs and tokens from various communities.\
- DYM token stakers are eligible for a majority of the airdrop tokens, with 50 million NIM in total.\
- The claim window for the airdrop runs until April 4.

news.headline: AI Gaming Blockchain Nim Network Launches Airdrop
news.summary:
- Nim Network, a blockchain focused on AI gaming, has launched its Genesis Rolldrop.\
- 9% of the NIM token supply will be airdropped to eligible users.\
- 50 million tokens can be claimed by Dymension (DYM) stakers.\
- NIM tokens have been allocated to popular gaming and NFT communities, as well as early adopters of infrastructure and public funding projects.\
- RollApps, which operate with the Dymension Hub, offer a more efficient and scalable user experience.\
- Users can claim their tokens once the chain goes live.

news.headline: Paradigm-backed Symbiotic unveils restaking protocol
news.summary:
- Restaking protocol Symbiotic has launched and raised $5.8 million in funding from Paradigm and cyber.Fund.\
- Symbiotic allows user deposits to secure third-party networks using a shared security model, similar to Ethereum's EigenLayer.\
- Depositors in Symbiotic will earn rewards for contributing to the security of third-party networks.\
- The protocol has a flexible and configurable modular design that supports any mix of tokens as restaked collateral.\
- Notable integrations include Ethena, Chainbound's Bolt, Hyperlane, Marlin's Kalypso, Fairblock, Ojo, Rollkit, and others.\
- EigenLayer, the first restaking protocol on Ethereum, has seen significant growth in total value locked since its launch.

news.headline: CoinDesk Market Index Adds Convex, Serum, 12 Other Digital Assets
Crypto Wallet MetaMask Expands From Ethereum and Solana to Bitcoin
news.summary:
- MetaMask has officially rolled out support for Bitcoin, enabling users to buy, send, and receive BTC directly within the wallet.\
- This expansion beyond Ethereum and Solana follows an initial announcement regarding Bitcoin integration nearly a year prior.\
- Users upgrading to the latest MetaMask version can now trade and manage BTC alongside assets from Ethereum, Solana, Monad, and Sei.\
- Traders who swap into BTC via MetaMask will earn reward points as part of a $30 million incentive program ahead of the wallet's native token launch.\
- Recent feature enhancements for the wallet also include launching its mUSD stablecoin, native support for perps trading on Hyperliquid, and a Polymarket integration for prediction markets.\
- MetaMask is scheduled to add further network support throughout 2026.

news.headline: Phantom’s Stablecoin CASH Supply Tops $100M Two Months Post-Launch
news.summary:
- Phantom’s native stablecoin, Cash (CASH), surpassed a $100 million supply two months after its September 30 launch.\
- Growth was spurred by the introduction of a fee-free "Cash Account" in mid-November, enabling instant bank funding and Stripe debit-card integration.\
- Protocol incentives, including the "CASH Growth Initiative" with Kamino offering KMNO rewards, also boosted demand.\
- CASH transaction volume peaked near 162,000 on November 25, according to Artemis data.\
- MetaMask’s stablecoin, MetaMask USD (mUSD), briefly topped $100 million in early October but has since fallen to approximately $25 million following the end of its liquidity programs.\
- mUSD is restricted to Ethereum and Linea networks, unlike CASH which operates across networks supported by Phantom, including Solana.

news.headline: Solana Name Service airdrops 40% of token supply after FIDA tokenomics deemed unsustainable
news.summary:
- Solana Name Service (SNS) launched a token generation event on May 13, with 40% of its supply allocated to early supporters, and tokens must be claimed by August 11.  \
- The new SNS token aims to replace the previously issued FIDA token, which Bonafida stated was unsustainable due to poor tokenomics.  \
- SNS is a decentralized naming protocol on Solana designed to simplify blockchain addresses with human-readable names like "yourname.sol."  \
- As of now, SNS is trading at $0.0048 with a market cap over $12 million, and 20% of the supply was reportedly spoken for earlier this month.  \
- The project emphasizes improving blockchain usability and reducing transactional errors by mapping complex addresses to simple names.  \
- Bonafida announced a new team and mission earlier in May, following FIDA's tokenomics issues.

news.headline: Solana Name Service to Airdrop 40% of Token Supply
news.summary:
- Solana Name Service (SNS) plans to airdrop 40% of its token supply to the community, with 20% distributed on launch day and 20% for participants in the upcoming “LFG Campaign.”  \
- The remaining 20% of tokens will be allocated for future emissions, ecosystem growth, liquidity, and team/investors, resulting in only 25% in circulation at launch.  \
- SNS’s tokenomics include a second token drop, with the FIDA token down 31% over the past week, now valued at a $56 million market cap.  \
- The SNS token will be significantly smaller than Ethereum Name Service (ENS), which has 798,000 active domains and 126,000 holders.  \
- The initial airdrop is among the most generous, with 20% of the supply distributed upfront and an additional 20% for the LFG Campaign, compared to typical distributions of 8-15%.  \
- No details have been released about the LFG Campaign’s requirements or how users can earn their share of the 20% token allocation.

news.headline: Real Estate Tokenization Firm Propy Eyes $100M U.S. Expansion to Modernize Title Industry
news.summary:
- Propy, a real estate tokenization platform, plans to raise $100M to expand in the U.S. and modernize the property title industry.  \
- The funding aims to streamline property transactions through blockchain, reducing paperwork and increasing transparency.  \
